title: [Feature] ViT Full CUDA Graph
body:
<!-- markdownlint-disable -->

## Purpose

Add full CUDA graph for the ViT to reduce kernel launch overheads.

**Features:**
  - **Budget-based graphs with a maximum batch size**:
      - Capture CUDA graphs at configurable token budgets (e.g., `[256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096]`).
      - Pad sequence metadata (e.g. cu_seqlen) so that we can use the same budget-based graph for various number of images
  during replays.
  - **Greedy bin-packing**:
      - Sort images in a batch in ascending order to reduce the number of graphs.
  - **Data-parallel (DP) support**:
      - When `mm_encoder_tp_mode=data`, each TP rank runs the ViT independently via data parallelism.
  - **FlashInfer cuDNN attention support**:
      - Override FlashInfer buckets in the CUDA graph path.
  - **Model-agnostic protocol**:
      - `SupportsEncoderCudaGraph` protocol in `interfaces.py` — models opt in by implementing 9 protocol methods for input
   handling, metadata computation, and forward dispatch.
      - `EncoderCudaGraphManager` is fully model-agnostic; all model-specific logic (grid config, dummy inputs, embedding
  computation) lives in the model class.

**New config flags** (via `--compilation-config`):
  - `cudagraph_mm_encoder: true` — enable encoder CUDA graph
  - `encoder_cudagraph_token_budgets: [...]` — list of token budget sizes to capture
  - `encoder_cudagraph_max_images_per_batch: N` — max images per graph replay

**Files changed:**
  - `vllm/config/compilation.py` — new config flags
  - `vllm/model_executor/models/interfaces.py` — `SupportsEncoderCudaGraph` protocol and `supports_encoder_cudagraph()`
  type guard
  - `vllm/model_executor/models/qwen3_vl.py` — implement `SupportsEncoderCudaGraph` on `Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration`
  - `vllm/v1/worker/gpu/mm/encoder_cudagraph_defs.py` — `EncoderCudaGraphConfig`, `EncoderCudaGraphCaptureInputs`,
  `EncoderCudaGraphReplayBuffers` dataclasses
  - `vllm/v1/worker/gpu/mm/encoder_cudagraph.py` — `EncoderCudaGraphManager` (capture, replay, packing, DP)
  - `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py` — integration into V1 model runner
  - `tests/v1/cudagraph/test_encoder_cudagraph.py` — unit and GPU tests

cc @maxyanghu @wangshangsam @Anerudhan

## Test Plan

**Unit Tests:**
```bash
pytest tests/v1/cudagraph/test_encoder_cudagraph.py -v
```

**End-to-End Tests**:
- **Single GPU**: Qwen3-VL-30B-A3B-Instruct, VisionArena-Chat dataset, 3000 prompts + 300 warmup
```
vllm bench mm-processor \
  --model Qwen/Qwen3-VL-30B-A3B-Instruct \
  --dataset-name hf --dataset-path lmarena-ai/VisionArena-Chat \
  --num-prompts 3000 --num-warmups 300 \
  --max-model-len 32768 --seed 42 \
  --mm-encoder-attn-backend FLASHINFER \
  --compilation-config '{"cudagraph_mm_encoder": true, "encoder_cudagraph_token_budgets": [512, 1024, 1536, 2048, 2560, 3072, 3584, 4096, 4864], "encoder_cudagraph_max_images_per_batch": 8}'
```
- **Multi GPU**: Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct, 4×GB200 TP=4 + ViT DP=4, random-mm dataset (20 imgs/req, 336×336), 1000 prompts + 200 warmup
```
vllm bench mm-processor \
  --model Qwen/Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct \
  --dataset-name random-mm \
  --random-mm-base-items-per-request 20 \
  --random-mm-num-mm-items-range-ratio 0.0 \
  --random-mm-bucket-config '{"(336,336,1)": 1.0}' \
  --num-prompts 1000 --num-warmups 200 \
  --max-model-len 8192 --seed 42 \
  --mm-encoder-attn-backend FLASHINFER \
  --tensor-parallel-size 4 --mm-encoder-tp-mode data \
  --compilation-config '{"cudagraph_mm_encoder": true, "encoder_cudagraph_token_budgets": [512, 1024, 1536, 2048, 2560, 3072, 3584, 4096, 4864], "encoder_cudagraph_max_images_per_batch": 8}'
```

## Test Result

**Single GPU** (Qwen3-VL-30B, 1×GB200, VisionArena-Chat, 3000 prompts):

| Backend | Mean | P99 |
|---|---|---|
| FLASH_ATTN | +11.8% (5.13→4.52ms) | +31.6% (9.16→6.26ms) |
| FLASH_ATTN | +19.6% (5.42→4.36ms) | +40.3% (10.87→6.49ms) |

**Multi GPU** (Qwen3-VL-32B, 4×GB200 TP=4 DP=4, random-mm 20img/req, 1000 prompts):

| Backend | Mean | P99 |
|---|---|---|
| FLASH_ATTN | +18.4% (28.39→23.16ms) | +14.0% (238.78→205.28ms) |
| FLASHINFER | +44.4% (23.24→12.91ms) | +84.9% (172.41→26.05ms) |

---
<details>
<summary> Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist </summary>

- [ ] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)".
- [ ] The test plan, such as providing test command.
- [ ] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results
- [ ] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model.
- [ ] (Optional) Release notes update. If your change is user facing, please update the release notes draft in the [Google Doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YyVqrgX4gHTtrstbq8oWUImOyPCKSGnJ7xtTpmXzlRs/edit?tab=t.0).
</details>
author: b-mu

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces full CUDA graph support for the Vision Transformer (ViT) encoder, aiming to reduce kernel launch overhead and improve performance, particularly in multi-GPU scenarios. The implementation is well-structured, featuring a budget-based graph capture system with greedy bin-packing for efficient batching of images. Key additions include new configuration flags for enabling and tuning the feature, data-parallel sharding utilities for multi-GPU vision processing, and a new `EncoderCudaGraphManager` that encapsulates the CUDA graph lifecycle management. The integration into the existing model runner is clean, and the necessary modifications to the Qwen3-VL model are minimal and well-justified. Furthermore, a comprehensive new test suite has been added to validate the functionality of the encoder CUDA graph manager, covering various scenarios including capture, replay, fallbacks, and data parallelism. Overall, this is a high-quality contribution that brings a significant performance enhancement.

 <sub>**Note:** Security Review did not run due to the size of the PR.</sub>

With a first glance, my concern is the generality of current encoder CG manager. I feel current implementation is too qwen3vl-specific (MRoPE + ViT RoPE), and it's difficult to boardcast this CG support to other ViTs.
As an experimental functionality, the model interface and cg manager overall LGTM now. But still have a minor suggestion about UX.

commits.message:
[Core] Add compute_encoder_metadata() utility for vision encoders

Add generic utility to compute sequence packing metadata (cu_seqlens,
sequence_lengths, max_seqlen) for vision encoders. Uses CPU computation
to avoid GPU sync issues.

Works for all standard grid-based ViT models using universal formula:
patches_per_image = T * (H // spatial_merge) * (W // spatial_merge)

Supports optional padding to fixed batch size for CUDA graph compatibility.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Config] Add CUDA graph config flags for vision encoders

Add three new configuration fields to CompilationConfig for vision encoder
CUDA graph support:

- cudagraph_mm_encoder: Enable/disable encoder CUDA graphs
- encoder_cudagraph_token_budgets: Token budget levels for graph capture
- encoder_cudagraph_max_images_per_batch: Max batch size for capture

Includes validation to ensure token budgets are specified when enabled
and max_images_per_batch is positive.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Core] Add EncoderCudaGraphManager infrastructure

Add budget-based CUDA graph manager for vision encoders with:
- BudgetGraphMetadata: stores graph and buffers for replay
- Budget selection logic (find smallest fitting budget)
- Stats tracking (hit/miss rates)
- Placeholder methods for capture/replay (to be implemented)

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Model] Add encoder_metadata kwarg to Qwen3 ViT forward

Modify Qwen3_VisionTransformer.forward() to accept optional encoder_metadata
for CUDA graph compatibility. When provided, uses pre-computed embeddings and
sequence metadata instead of computing them eagerly.

Backward compatible: eager path unchanged (encoder_metadata=None by default).

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Core] Add per_frame parameter to compute_encoder_metadata

Support two sequence packing modes:
- per_frame=False: Whole-sequence (T*H*W per video/image) - lfm2_vl style
- per_frame=True: Per-frame sequences (H*W repeated T times) - Qwen style

Add guard for padding with videos in per_frame mode (only images supported).

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Core] Implement CUDA graph capture for encoder

Implement _capture_budget_graph() with:
- Generate dummy grid config and inputs for capture
- Compute position/rotary embeddings and sequence metadata
- Warmup run to determine output shape
- CUDA graph capture with torch.cuda.graph()
- Store graph and buffers in BudgetGraphMetadata

Add helper methods:
- _generate_grid_config_for_budget(): Create dummy grids for token budget
- _prepare_dummy_inputs(): Generate dummy pixel_values tensor

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Core] Implement encoder CUDA graph replay

Copy inputs/metadata into buffers, replay graph, return output.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Core] Integrate EncoderCudaGraphManager into runtime

Add execute() method to manager for runtime inference.
Wire into gpu_model_runner initialization, capture, and dispatch.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Core] Add DPVisionShardingMeta dataclass

Metadata container for data-parallel vision encoder sharding.
Used by dp_shard/gather utilities to distribute and reconstruct outputs.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Core] Add dp_shard_vision_inputs utility

Load-balance and distribute vision inputs across TP ranks.
Returns local inputs and metadata for gathering.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Core] Add dp_gather_vision_outputs utility

All-gather outputs from TP ranks and reconstruct in original order.
Handles padding/unpadding for all-gather operation.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Core] Add DP support to EncoderCudaGraphManager

Detect mm_encoder_tp_mode=data and use dp_shard/gather utilities.
Refactor execute() to support both DP and non-DP paths.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Fix] Fix runtime issues for CUDA graph capture

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Fix] Use rectangular grids for exact encoder CUDA graph budgets

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Enhancement] Add stats logging for encoder CUDA graph execution

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Fix] Fix max_seqlen and spatial_merge_size for encoder CUDA graph

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Fix] Fix encoder CUDA graph case when num_images > max_batch_size

Split oversized batches into max_batch_size chunks, each processed via
CUDA graph with eager fallback. Also count graph hits/misses per image.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Refactor] Extract token count helpers in encoder_cudagraph

Add _count_input_patches() and _count_output_tokens() module-level
helpers to replace repeated inline formulas in _execute_local and
_prepare_dummy_inputs.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Test] Add unit tests for encoder CUDA graph manager

- test_vision.py: compute_encoder_metadata (grid sizes, per_frame, padding)
- test_encoder_cudagraph.py: budget selection, token counting, grid config
  generation, and hit/miss stats (no GPU required)

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Test] Add GPU tests for EncoderCudaGraphManager capture/replay

- SimpleMockViTEncoder: minimal ViT mock implementing the interface
  expected by EncoderCudaGraphManager
- TestEncoderCudaGraphCaptureReplay: graph capture, output shape,
  budget fallback, hit/miss counters, and oversized-batch chunking

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Refactor] Rename _find_budget_graph to _find_smallest_fitting_budget_given_tokens

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Enhancement] Add greedy packing to encoder CUDA graph execution

Sort images by output token count (smallest first), then greedily pack
as many images as possible into each batch bounded by max_budget tokens
and max_batch_size. The smallest fitting budget is found once per
finalised batch. Oversized images (> max_budget) fall back to eager
internally. By exchange argument, greedy smallest-first packing minimises
eager fallbacks for mixed image-size batches.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Enhancement] Add FlashInfer cuDNN support for encoder CUDA graph

Add _compute_encoder_metadata() helper that pads cu_seqlens to
max_batch_size once, then applies FlashInfer transforms (scale +
qko/v split) directly — bypassing FLASHINFER_BATCH_BUCKETS which
exist only to limit cuDNN recompilation in eager mode.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Refactor] Split metadata_buffers into embed_buffers and sequence_metadata_buffers

Separate BudgetGraphMetadata.metadata_buffers into two fields to
distinguish position embeddings from sequence metadata. Rename
_compute_encoder_metadata() to _compute_sequence_metadata().

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Cleanup] Remove unused compute_encoder_metadata()

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Refactor] Add encoder CUDA graph dataclasses

Add EncoderCudaGraphConfig, EncoderCudaGraphCaptureInputs, and
EncoderCudaGraphReplayBuffers data transfer objects that will be
used by the SupportsEncoderCudaGraph protocol and the refactored
EncoderCudaGraphManager.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Refactor] Add SupportsEncoderCudaGraph protocol

Define the SupportsEncoderCudaGraph protocol in interfaces.py with
9 methods that models must implement to opt into encoder CUDA graph
capture/replay. Includes supports_encoder_cudagraph() type guard.

The protocol encapsulates all model-specific logic (input handling,
metadata computation, forward pass) so the EncoderCudaGraphManager
can be model-agnostic.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Refactor] Implement SupportsEncoderCudaGraph on Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration

Move all Qwen3-VL-specific CUDA graph logic (grid config generation,
dummy input creation, embedding computation, sequence metadata, forward
dispatch) into protocol methods on the model class. This encapsulates
model internals that were previously accessed directly by the manager.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Refactor] Refactor EncoderCudaGraphManager to use SupportsEncoderCudaGraph protocol

Rewrite the manager to be model-agnostic by delegating all model-specific
logic to protocol methods. Move DP sharding/gathering into the manager
using generic get_load_balance_assignment() and protocol methods.

- encoder_cudagraph.py: Take SupportsEncoderCudaGraph model instead of
  raw vision_model, use protocol for capture/replay/packing/DP
- gpu_model_runner.py: Use supports_encoder_cudagraph() type guard and
  supports_modality() check instead of hardcoded Qwen3-VL assumptions
- vision.py: Remove PR-added Qwen-specific DP utilities superseded by
  the manager's generic DP implementation
- test_encoder_cudagraph.py: Update mock model to implement protocol,
  adapt tests to new mm_kwargs-based API

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Bugfix] Unwrap CUDAGraphWrapper before SupportsEncoderCudaGraph check

When decoder CUDA graphs are enabled (the default FULL_AND_PIECEWISE
mode), self.model is wrapped in CUDAGraphWrapper before the encoder
CUDA graph manager is initialized. Python's @runtime_checkable Protocol
isinstance() does not resolve attributes through __getattr__, so the
check always returned False and the encoder CUDA graph manager was
never created.

Fix by using self.get_model() to unwrap the CUDAGraphWrapper before
the protocol check.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Bugfix] Fix tensor-scalar aliasing and output buffer overwrite in encoder CUDA graph

Two bugs in EncoderCudaGraphManager._execute_local():

1. per_item_out_tokens could contain 0-dim tensor scalars (when image_grid_thw
   arrives as CPU tensors via keep_on_cpu=True). In the greedy packing loop,
   `current_batch_tokens = item_tokens` aliases the tensor, and subsequent
   `+=` mutates it in-place via __iadd__, corrupting per_item_out_tokens
   and causing batches to exceed their budget. Fix: convert to plain ints.

2. Output slices from _run_budget_graph are views into a shared output_buffer.
   When multiple batches use the same budget graph, later replays overwrite
   earlier results. Fix: clone output slices.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Refactor] Extract prepare_encoder_metadata() to remove duplicates in encoder metadata computation

Add a shared prepare_encoder_metadata() method to Qwen3_VisionTransformer
that consolidates the duplicated metadata computation (pos_embeds, rotary
embeddings, cu_seqlens, max_seqlen, sequence_lengths) across the eager
forward path, CUDA graph capture, and CUDA graph replay.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Refactor] Extract helpers to remove duplicates in encoder CUDA graph manager

Extract _get_per_item_out_tokens() and _scatter_output_slices() to
consolidate duplicated per-item token computation and output tensor
slicing across _execute_local(), execute(), and _dp_gather().

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[CI] Fix ruff formatting and line length

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Refactor] Move imports to module level in encoder CUDA graph manager

Move distributed and vision imports from local function scope to module
level for consistency and cleaner code.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

[Feature] Auto-infer encoder CUDA graph token budgets and max images per batch

Add get_encoder_cudagraph_budget_range() protocol method so models can
provide (min_budget, max_budget) for automatic budget generation.
When encoder_cudagraph_token_budgets is empty or
encoder_cudagraph_max_images_per_batch is 0, the manager auto-infers
from the model. User-provided values always override auto-inference.

Signed-off-by: Baorun Mu <bmu@nvidia.com>

title: [Bugfix] Fix Hermes tool parser when stream interval > 1
body:
## Purpose
The Hermes parser tried to interpret each `delta_text` independently, using a token buffer to reconstruct `<tool_call>` tag boundaries and `partial_json_parser` to parse incomplete JSON. This broke when `stream_interval > 1` caused tokens to arrive in multi-token chunks.

### The fix
**Parses `current_text` on every call and diffs against what was already sent.** This makes it chunk-size-independent.

```
current_text = "Sure!<tool_call>{\"name\":\"f\",\"arguments\":{\"x\":1"
                ├ content ┤├──────── tool call region (partial) ────────┤
                Step 1      Step 2 & 3
```

| Step | What | How | Emits |
|------|------|-----|-------|
| 1 | Extract content | Text before `<tool_call>`, hold back partial tag suffix, diff vs `_sent_content_idx` | `DeltaMessage(content=new_chars)` |
| 2 | Parse tool regions | Find all `<tool_call>JSON</tool_call>` pairs → `[(json_body, is_complete), ...]` | *(intermediate — nothing emitted)* |
| 3 | Diff & emit tool name & args | Name unsent? → regex extract. Args grew? → string diff vs `streamed_args_for_tool[i]` | `DeltaToolCall(name=...)` / `DeltaToolCall(arguments=new_suffix)` |

## Test Plan
Unit test:
```
python -m pytest tests/tool_parsers/test_hermes_tool_parser.py -v
```
Manual e2e test:
```
# Start server
vllm serve Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct \
      --tool-call-parser hermes \
      --enable-auto-tool-choice \
      --stream-interval 1/5/10

Ran script: https://gist.github.com/sfeng33/d829104a4e70c07895266395f64da632
```
author: sfeng33

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request significantly refactors the streaming tool call parsing logic for the Hermes2ProToolParser, replacing the previous `partial_json_parser` based approach with a more robust, incremental parsing mechanism. This involves introducing new helper functions for handling partial tool call tags, validating JSON, and extracting tool names and arguments. The `extract_tool_calls_streaming` method has been completely rewritten to leverage this new logic, ensuring that only new content or tool call fragments are emitted during streaming. Comprehensive new tests have been added to validate the streaming behavior under various conditions and stream intervals. Additionally, similar token-related attributes were removed from the LongcatToolParser. A key feedback point highlighted a potential bug where the parser's internal state (`_sent_content_idx`, `prev_tool_call_arr`, `streamed_args_for_tool`) could leak across multiple requests if not explicitly reset, suggesting a fix to clear this state at the beginning of each new request.

## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.
I’ll need a bit more time for this PR since Hermes is the most widely used tool parser.
LGTM

I gave this some close scrutiny as well, given how widely this tool call parser is used. This should fix a whole class of bugs related to parsing with the `delta_text` before vs the `current_text` now.

Overall things look great, but I'd like to request one change. I believe, as written now, this would break for large stream_interval values if we got both content and a tool call within a single delta. I could be wrong, so the change I'd like to request is a test for this that includes a small bit of content and an entire valid tool call in a single delta chunk just to confirm that we don't regress there, as I believe the previous code would continue to parse tool calls even after finding content in a delta.

Thanks for tackling this!
That was an elegant fix for the content + tool call in one chunk with large stream_intervals. And, nice test. This feels really solid, and giving it an approval. Thanks!

title: [Refactor] Move FusedMoE hidden_size roundup to quant_method
body:
- Refactor hidden_size and intermediate roundup logic to be handled by QuantMethod.
- Store padded and unpadded sizes under MoeConfig.
- ~~Update ROCm padding logic to improve performance on mi300x. Thanks for suggestion and evaluation from @Rohan138.~~ moved to separate pr #38043
- Enable Quark MXFP4 MoE with aiter backend running with padded intermediate_size.
author: BowenBao

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request refactors the logic for rounding up the `hidden_size` in FusedMoE layers, moving the responsibility from the generic `FusedMoE` layer to the specific quantization methods. This is a good architectural improvement. My main feedback is about code duplication and a potential bug in `QuarkOCP_MX_MoEMethod` where the roundup logic is applied unconditionally for `gpt_oss` models, even for non-MXFP4 quantization types.

## Code Review

This pull request refactors the logic for rounding up the hidden size in FusedMoE layers by moving it from the generic `layer.py` to the specific `quant_method` implementations. This is a good architectural improvement, as it places quantization-specific logic where it belongs. The changes in `fused_moe_method_base.py` and `layer.py` are correct. However, this refactoring has introduced code duplication in `mxfp4.py` and `quark_moe.py` for handling `gpt_oss` models. I've added comments with suggestions to address this.

## Code Review

This pull request refactors the `hidden_size` roundup logic for FusedMoE layers by moving it into the `quant_method`. This is a good architectural improvement as it localizes quantization-specific logic. The changes are well-structured. I've found one issue where a function is called with incorrect arguments, which I've detailed in a specific comment.

## Code Review

This pull request refactors the `hidden_size` and `intermediate_size` rounding logic in the `FusedMoE` layer by moving it into the `quant_method`. This is a significant improvement in maintainability as it centralizes quantization-specific alignment requirements (especially for MXFP4 backends) within the quantization methods themselves, rather than having brittle model-type checks in the core layer logic. The changes ensure that both the `moe_config` and the actual weight tensors are created with consistent, correctly padded dimensions across different hardware platforms and quantization schemes.
Left some comments FYI.
LGTM, thanks for addressing the comments.
LGTM. OCP MX emulation should be refactored as an Mxfp4Backend in a follow up PR

The padding change moved to its own PR at #38043 
Could you please remove it here to avoid conflicts. I think it's GTG otherwise

title: Make Qwen3VL compatible with Transformers v5
body:
The location of `tie_word_embeddings` has moved for Transformers v5 for multimodal models to a more sensible location (please see https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/33359 for the full explanation).

This PR updates the Qwen3VL implementation to leverage the fix made in https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/33359 and moves the `vision_config` specific check that was taking place in `Qwen3LLMModel` to `Qwen3VLForConditionalGeneration`, where the vision config is available.
author: hmellor

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request successfully updates the Qwen3VL implementation to be compatible with Transformers v5, addressing the relocation of `tie_word_embeddings` and moving the `vision_config` specific check to the appropriate class. The changes also include necessary dependency updates and a transition from `hf_transfer` to `hf-xet` for faster Hugging Face downloads. The refactoring of the vision configuration checks improves code organization and correctness for multimodal models.

<details>
<summary>I am having trouble creating individual review comments. Click here to see my feedback.</summary>

### requirements/nightly_torch_test.txt (32)

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

Pinning `transformers` to the `main` branch of the Hugging Face repository introduces a moving target dependency. This can lead to unexpected build failures or behavioral changes if the `main` branch introduces breaking changes or is rebased. For stability and reproducibility, it is highly recommended to pin `transformers` to a specific stable release version or a fixed commit hash that is guaranteed not to change.

```
transformers==5.0.0
```

### requirements/test.txt (1232)

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

While pinning `transformers` to a specific commit hash (`520fad98fe370c69807481e2cf2e2dce946f9374`) is better than relying on the `main` branch, it still depends on a non-release version. This can lead to issues if the upstream repository re-writes history (e.g., rebase) or if this specific commit is removed. For production-grade stability, it's best to use a version from a stable release of `transformers`.

```
transformers==5.0.0
```

</details>
@hmellor Thanks for the update - does this mean after this change the model won't work with transformers v4?
I fixed it for Qwen3VL MoE and confirmed the model can launch on both V4 and V5 - let's get this in!

- [ ] (Optional) Release notes update. If your change is user facing, please update the release notes draft in the [Google Doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YyVqrgX4gHTtrstbq8oWUImOyPCKSGnJ7xtTpmXzlRs/edit?tab=t.0).
</details>
author: bigPYJ1151

reviews.body:
## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

## Code Review

This pull request extends the CPU mixed-precision linear kernel to support new quantization formats, including a "CT format" which involves specific weight, scale, and zero-point transpositions. The weight processing logic has been refactored for greater generality, utilizing `getattr` for attribute access and dynamically determining the quantization method (GPTQ vs. AWQ). A new test model with zero points (`RedHatAI/Qwen3-1.7B-quantized.w4a16`) has been added. A high-severity concern was raised regarding potential out-of-memory errors during model loading due to the `unpack_quantized_values_into_int32` function creating a large intermediate tensor; chunking the weight conversion is suggested as an optimization.

The implementation looks logically sound, but the PR description is missing test plan and test results — please provide output from running the CPU quantization tests before this merges.


<details>
<summary>Extended reasoning...</summary>

### Overview
This PR modifies cpu.py to support Compressed Tensor (CT) W4A16 format on the CPU MP kernel, alongside the existing marlin/GPTQ and AWQ formats. A new test model is added to the CPU quantization test suite.

### Code Analysis
The core change detects CT format by checking packed_weight.input_dim == 1 (vs marlin input_dim == 0), transposes the CT tensor to marlin-like layout, then applies a shared weight transformation. The new unpack(..., packed_dim=0) path is mathematically equivalent to the old unpack(..., packed_dim=1) + view + permute path for marlin format. The attribute access change (getattr(layer, self.w_q_name) replacing layer.qweight) correctly uses base class attribute names. The routing change from zero_points-based to dimension-based is sound since AWQ uses marlin-style packing (input_dim == packed_dim == 0).

### Security Risks
No security concerns — this is purely a CPU kernel weight transformation for quantized model inference.

### Level of Scrutiny
Moderate. The weight transformation logic changes affect all quantized model inference on CPU (GPTQ, AWQ, and now CT). While mathematical equivalence for existing formats is provable, this code path requires physical CPU hardware to test.

### Other Factors
The PR description checklist is entirely unchecked — test plan and test results sections are empty. For a CPU-specific kernel change that needs hardware validation, human review with confirmed test results is the right gate before merging.

</details>

</details>
author: wxsIcey

reviews.body:
## Code Review

The Gated Delta Net (GDN) attention implementation, including its custom operations and Triton kernels, has been refactored into a new dedicated file, `gdn_linear_attn.py`. This new `GatedDeltaNetAttention` class now serves as a unified implementation for both Qwen3-Next and Qwen3.5 models, replacing the previously separate GDN classes and handling model-specific configurations like GQA interleaved layouts and LoRA compatibility through parameters. A critical issue was identified in the `fix_query_key_value_ordering` method, where the `new_tensor_shape_ba` is incorrectly derived from `mixed_qkvz.size()` instead of `mixed_ba.size()`, which could lead to a runtime error if the number of tokens differs between these tensors.
Thanks for refactoring. just some minor comments from my side.

A few things still need attention before this can merge: there is an unresolved reviewer question on gdn_linear_attn.py, pre-commit was failing as of March 26 (unclear if fixed), and the gdn_in_proj stub calls an undefined _forward_in_proj method (see inline comment).


<details>
<summary>Extended reasoning...</summary>

### Overview
This PR extracts GatedDeltaNetAttention from qwen3_next.py into a new shared gdn_linear_attn.py and removes the Qwen3NextGatedDeltaNet/Qwen3_5GatedDeltaNet subclass hierarchy in favour of a single parameterised class. It touches ~1000 lines across three files, primarily a refactor but with meaningful behavioural changes.

### Security Risks
No security-sensitive code paths are touched. Risk is limited to model correctness and inference performance.

### Level of Scrutiny
The change is architecturally meaningful as the foundation for out-of-tree platform dispatch unifying two previously divergent implementations. LM Eval on B200 passed, but several reviewer concerns remain open. This warrants human approval rather than bot shadow-approval.

### Other Factors
1. **Unresolved reviewer comment** - The inline question on gdn_linear_attn.py (comment id 2981910661, "is this necessary?") is not marked resolved in the timeline.
2. **Pre-commit failure** - Mergify reported a pre-commit failure on March 26; it is not confirmed whether this was subsequently fixed.
3. **gdn_in_proj dead code** - The stub function at line 950 calls self._forward_in_proj which does not exist on GatedDeltaNetAttention. While currently unreachable dead code, it is a footgun for future platform-plugin authors (see inline comment for details).
4. **forward_native naming** - ChunkGatedDeltaRule.forward_native actually invokes a Triton/FLA kernel, violating the CustomOp convention. The team agreed to defer this to a follow-up.

</details>

title: [kv_offload+HMA][7/N]: Support register_kv_caches for hybrid models
body:
This PR extends the offloading connector `register_kv_caches` function to support KV caches used in hybrid models.

We define a new `CanonicalKVCaches` class which captures:
1. The unique set of KV cache tensors (as tensors maybe shared by multiple layers)
2. Mapping each group to its relevant KV cache data (given by a tensor pointer + page size). The canonical tensors are each of dtype int8 and shape `(num_blocks, page_size)`.

This PR also splits the offloading connector unit tests to multiple files.
author: orozery

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a significant and well-executed refactoring to support KV cache offloading for hybrid models. The introduction of `CanonicalKVCaches` provides a clean abstraction over different KV cache layouts, improving modularity and simplifying the transfer handler logic. The accompanying test refactoring is also a good improvement.

I have one high-severity comment regarding an inconsistency in the data type of the canonicalized tensor, which could lead to maintenance issues in the future. Addressing this would make the new abstraction more robust and easier to reason about.

hey @orozery , left only a few comments here mainly because I can understand what you're doing, but I am afraid I don't fully understand _why_ you're doing it.

Could you elaborate on why we would need a CanonicalKVCacheTensor here and how is that abstraction making life easier for you in code/transfer?

>All cases need to assign to tensors_per_block, page_size_bytes and unpadded_page_size_bytes.

Yes that's the pattern I would like to factor out..
Possibly the canonical torch.tensor/raw creation in particular which is quite verbose.

Anyways I am unblocking given this is minor and could maybe be simply wrapped in a util or constructor method for `CanonicalKVCaches`, in order to keep core logic in worker file as lean as possible.

I'll leave it to you to shape as you see fit for best maintainability.

commits.message:
[kv_offload+HMA][7/N]: Support register_kv_caches for hybrid models

This commit extends the offloading connector register_kv_caches function
to support KV caches used in hybrid models.
We define a new CanonicalKVCaches class which captures:
1. The unique set of KV cache tensors (as tensors maybe shared by multiple layers)
2. Mapping each group to its relevant KV cache data (given by a tensor pointer + page size).
The canonical tensors are each of dtype int8 and shape (num_blocks, page_size).
This commit also splits the offloading connector unit tests to multiple files.

Signed-off-by: Or Ozeri <oro@il.ibm.com>

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a fused Triton kernel, `fused_sigmoid_gating_delta_rule_update`, which combines sigmoid gating and the delta rule update to optimize performance by avoiding the materialization of intermediate tensors. The new kernel is well-implemented and correctly integrated into the `Qwen3Next` model for both speculative and non-speculative decoding paths, showing performance improvements as per the benchmarks. My review includes one high-severity suggestion to improve the new unit tests by aligning tensor data types with their usage in the model, ensuring the tests are more robust and representative.
LGTM

title: fix(glm47): improve tool call parsing and content normalization
body:
## Summary

- **Improve GLM-4.7 `func_detail_regex`**: Use `\S+?` instead of `.*?` for the function name capture group, and make the arg group greedy (`.*` vs `.*?`) so all argument pairs are captured correctly. This produces cleaner function names without trailing whitespace/newlines.
- **Simplify `func_arg_regex`**: Replace redundant `(?:\\n|\s)*` with `\s*` between `</arg_key>` and `<arg_value>` tags.
- **Normalize empty content to `None`**: In `Glm4MoeModelToolParser.extract_tool_calls`, return `content=None` instead of `content=""` when there is no meaningful text before the tool call. This aligns with the OpenAI API convention where `content` is null when the assistant only produces tool calls.
- **Add GLM-4.7-specific tests**: New test file covering zero-argument tool calls, inline args (no newline between name and args), newline-separated args, multiple tool calls, content normalization, and streaming scenarios.
- **Update existing GLM-4.5 tests**: Fix `expected_content` values from `""` to `None` to match the content normalization change.

## Test plan
- [ ] Existing GLM-4.5 parser tests pass with updated expected values
- [ ] New GLM-4.7 parser tests cover all reported failure scenarios from #37277, #32436, #33877
- [ ] `pre-commit run --all-files` passes

Fixes #37277
Related: #32436, #33877
author: karanb192

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request improves tool call parsing for GLM-4.7 models by refining the regular expressions to handle different formatting and capture function names and arguments more robustly. It also introduces content normalization to align with OpenAI API conventions by treating empty or whitespace-only content as `None`. The changes are well-supported by new, specific tests for GLM-4.7 and updates to existing tests, ensuring the modifications are correct and don't introduce regressions. The overall changes enhance correctness and maintainability.
LGTM.

commits.message:
fix(glm47): improve tool call parsing and content normalization

Fix GLM-4.7 tool call parser regex and normalize empty content to
None for OpenAI API compatibility.

- Improve func_detail_regex to cleanly separate function name from
  args using \S+? instead of .*? to avoid trailing whitespace
- Simplify func_arg_regex by replacing redundant (?:\\n|\s)* with \s*
- Normalize empty/whitespace-only content to None in extract_tool_calls
- Update GLM-4.5 parser tests for the content normalization
- Add GLM-4.7 parser tests for zero-arg calls, inline args, streaming

Fixes: vllm-project/vllm#37277
Related: vllm-project/vllm#32436, vllm-project/vllm#33877
Signed-off-by: karanb192 <karan@example.com>

fix(glm47): fix streaming test for incremental string arg handling

Fix test_with_args streaming test that failed because city is declared
as a string type, triggering incremental string streaming.  Split the
arg value chunk so the parser processes <arg_value>, the value content,
and </arg_value> in separate calls, allowing </tool_call> to be
processed in the final call.  Also remove unused FunctionCall/ToolCall
import and apply ruff formatting.

Signed-off-by: karanb192 <karan@example.com>

title: Various Transformers v5 config fixes
body:
- Step 3.5 has more `layer_types` than `num_hidden_layers`, which fails validation. This PR crops `layer_types` for this model
- Changes the `HFConfigParser` to register custom config classes then use `AutoConfig` so that the same class is used elsewhere in vLLM (i.e. for tokenizers, which before this PR were not using the classes from `_CONFIG_REGISTRY`
- Move `super().__init__()` to the end of `__init__()` because it performs validation which will fail if the rest of the class hasn't been initialised yet
- Revert DeepSeekVLV2 change from https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/38127 because it breaks NIXL tests
author: hmellor

reviews.body:

## Code Review

This pull request refactors the configuration parsing logic in `vllm/transformers_utils/config.py` to consistently use `AutoConfig.from_pretrained` after registering custom model types. It also adjusts the initialization order in several model configuration classes (ColModernVBERT, DeepseekV2, FlexOlmo, Isaac, Qwen3Next) by moving the `super().__init__` call to a different position within their `__init__` methods, likely to ensure proper attribute initialization. Additionally, in `vllm/transformers_utils/configs/step3p5.py`, logic was added to truncate `layer_types` based on `num_hidden_layers`, but a debugging `print` statement was left in the code.

title: [Model Runner V2] Enable forcing a specific acceptance rate during rejection sampling
body:
## Purpose Enable testing/debugging with a fixed, expected acceptance rate. To easily support this in MRV2, I added a rejection sampling method to the speculative config (synthetic), and added a field for setting the desired, mean, acceptance rate (`synthetic_acceptance_rate`). That field is only used if `synthetic` rejection sampling is selected. In practice with speculative decoding with > 1 draft tokens, we see a drop off after each position. To simulate this drop-off, the base acceptance rate (position = 0) and decay factor are computed based on the desired, mean acceptance rate. Those are used to compute the per-position conditional acceptance rates, which are used for accepting/rejecting draft tokens. This is all to add realism to the results. The decay factor (minimum value of 0.85, what is generally seen in practice for well-tuned draft models) has to be selected carefully to ensure that the base acceptance rate does not exceed 1, which would prevent from achieving the desired mean acceptance rate. That is what `compute_base_acceptance_rate` and `min_valid_decay_factor` do. ## Test Plan Added test to ensure that the base acceptance rate and decay factor are computed correctly, and that the average of the joint probabilities in fact equals the desired value. ``` (vllm) gdelfin@h200-1:~/vllm-main$ pytest tests/v1/spec_decode/test_synthetic_rejection_sampler_utils.py -v ================================================== test session starts ================================================== platform linux -- Python 3.12.12, pytest-9.0.2, pluggy-1.6.0 -- /home/gdelfin/vllm/.venv/bin/python3 cachedir: .pytest_cache rootdir: /home/gdelfin/vllm-main configfile: pyproject.toml plugins: anyio-4.12.1 collected 7 items tests/v1/spec_decode/test_synthetic_rejection_sampler_utils.py::test_compute_synthetic_rejection_sampler_params[1] PASSED [ 14%] tests/v1/spec_decode/test_synthetic_rejection_sampler_utils.py::test_compute_synthetic_rejection_sampler_params[2] PASSED [ 28%] tests/v1/spec_decode/test_synthetic_rejection_sampler_utils.py::test_compute_synthetic_rejection_sampler_params[3] PASSED [ 42%] tests/v1/spec_decode/test_synthetic_rejection_sampler_utils.py::test_compute_synthetic_rejection_sampler_params[4] PASSED [ 57%] tests/v1/spec_decode/test_synthetic_rejection_sampler_utils.py::test_compute_synthetic_rejection_sampler_params[5] PASSED [ 71%] tests/v1/spec_decode/test_synthetic_rejection_sampler_utils.py::test_compute_synthetic_rejection_sampler_params[7] PASSED [ 85%] tests/v1/spec_decode/test_synthetic_rejection_sampler_utils.py::test_compute_synthetic_rejection_sampler_params[10] PASSED [100%] =================================================== 7 passed in 1.09s =================================================== ``` Additionally I manually tested using spec decoding with Llama3: **Server** ``` VLLM_USE_V2_MODEL_RUNNER=1 vllm serve meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct --no-enable-prefix-caching --tensor-parallel-size 1 --data-parallel-size 1 --speculative-config '{"method": "eagle", "model": "yuhuili/EAGLE-LLaMA3-Instruct-8B", "num_speculative_tokens": 3, "rejection_sample_method": "synthetic", "synthetic_acceptance_rate": }' ``` **Benchmark** ``` vllm bench serve --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct --tokenizer meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct --host 0.0.0.0 --dataset-name hf --dataset-path philschmid/mt-bench --ignore-eos --request-rate inf --max-concurrency 16 --temperature 0 ``` **Results**

Speculative Decoding Benchmark Results

Metric | Target 0.24 | Target 0.69 | Target 1.0 -- | -- | -- | -- Acceptance rate (%) | 23.98 | 69.06 | 100.00 Acceptance length | 1.72 | 3.07 | 4.00 Drafts | 148,650 | 83,405 | 64,000 Draft tokens | 445,950 | 250,215 | 192,000 Accepted tokens | 106,952 | 172,806 | 192,000 Per-position acceptance (%) |   |   |   Position 0 | 46.92 | 90.86 | 100.00 Position 1 | 18.62 | 70.20 | 100.00 Position 2 | 6.41 | 46.13 | 100.00
author: TheEpicDolphin

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a new "synthetic" rejection sampling method for speculative decoding, including its configuration and implementation. The `RejectionSampler` class has been updated to support this new method, alongside existing "strict" and "probabilistic" methods. A review comment suggests replacing an `assert` statement with a `ValueError` for validating the `synthetic_acceptance_rate` to ensure robust error handling.

- Way too much complexity here for an optional feature.
- I don't like the idea of skipping the main rejection sampling and replacing it with a synthetic kernel. If we are using synthetic sampling to approximate a workload, I would prefer to still call the main rejection sampler and then override the outputs so that we never underestimate the cost of the sampling itself
@benchislett The PR looks good to me. Can you take another look?

commits.message:
[Model Runner V2] Enable forcing a specific acceptance rate during rejection sampling

Signed-off-by: Giancarlo Delfin <gdelfin@inferact.ai>

title: Fix multi-node allreduce fusion
body:
## Purpose
The flashinfer trtllm allreduce backend does not work for multi-node setup (See issue https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/issues/2006). As a result, running allreduce fusion results in hang in multi-node. This PR resolves this by auto-selecting mnnvl backend for flashinfer all reduce in multi-node setup. In single node setup, the default backend remains trtllm due to issue #35772

## Test Plan
Test with nvidia/DeepSeek-R1-NVFP4 on the following setup:
1 x GB300 Node - single-node TP4
2 x GB300 Node - multi-node TP8

## Test Result
single-node TP4:
```
vllm serve nvidia/DeepSeek-R1-NVFP4 --no-enable-prefix-caching \
  --tensor-parallel-size 8 
```

```
[flashinfer_all_reduce.py:109] Auto-selected flashinfer allreduce backend: trtllm
[flashinfer_all_reduce.py:149] Initialized FlashInfer Allreduce norm fusion workspace with backend=trtllm
```

|     |       |strict-match    |     5|exact_match|↑  |0.9538|±  |0.0058|
```

multi-node TP8:
```
bash "$HOME/vllm/examples/online_serving/multi-node-serving.sh" \
    leader \
    --ray_port=6379 \
    --ray_cluster_size=2 && \
  vllm serve nvidia/DeepSeek-R1-NVFP4 --no-enable-prefix-caching \

```

```

title: [Bugfix][ROCm] Fix lru_cache on paged_mqa_logits_module
body:
Fix lru_cache on paged_mqa_logits_module by moving to module scope

<!-- markdownlint-disable -->

## Purpose

The function `paged_mqa_logits_module ` was defined inside `rocm_fp8_paged_mqa_logits`, causing a new function object (and thus a new empty cache) to be created on every call, defeating the purpose of lru_cache.
As a consequence, the module was reimported on each call.

The issue is in the ROCm Sparse MLA implementation, affecting DeepSeek v3.2.

## Test Plan

Benchmark using `vllm bench serve`

## Test Result

</details>
author: gronsti-amd

reviews.body:
## Code Review

The pull request correctly addresses the issue where `lru_cache` was ineffective on `paged_mqa_logits_module` by moving its definition to the module scope. This ensures that the cache is properly utilized, preventing redundant module imports and improving performance as intended. The change is a direct and effective fix for the described bug.
LGTM

title: [ROCm][CI] Override PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH with detected GPU arch in test containers
body:
The base Docker image sets `PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH` to all supported architectures for build-time compilation. This value persists as a runtime env var. When Quark's JIT kernel compilation runs during tests, it picks up this broad list and compiles for all architectures instead of just the GPU present on the machine.

Quark has auto-detection logic (`set_rocm_user_architecture`) but it only activates when `PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH` is unset. Since the Docker image always sets it, auto-detection is skipped.

## Change

In `run-amd-test.sh`, detect the host GPU arch using `rocm_agent_enumerator` (filtering out `gfx000` which represents the CPU) and override `PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH` via `docker run -e`. Falls back to `gfx90a;gfx942;gfx950` if detection fails.

cc @kenroche
author: AndreasKaratzas

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces dynamic detection of the GPU architecture using `rocm_agent_enumerator` to set the `PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH` environment variable for Docker containers, optimizing JIT compilation. A potential issue was identified where the `grep -v` command in the architecture detection pipeline could cause the script to exit prematurely under certain shell configurations, and a more robust approach was suggested.

## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

title: [ROCm][CI] Add uv pip compile workflow for rocm-test.txt lockfile
body:
Adds a proper `uv pip compile` workflow for generating `rocm-test.txt` from `rocm-test.in`, matching the existing CUDA `test.in` in to `test.txt` pattern.

### Changes
- Added `pip-compile rocm-test` hook in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` that compiles `rocm-test.in` in to `rocm-test.txt` using `-c requirements/rocm.txt` as a constraint file (since `--torch-backend rocm` doesn't exist in uv). CUDA/NVIDIA packages are excluded via `--no-emit-package` since ROCm torch is installed separately via `rocm.txt`.
- Added a verification step that asserts `torch.version.hip is not None` to catch accidental CUDA torch installation during the test image build.
- Regenerated as a proper uv-compiled lockfile (346 pinned packages, zero CUDA/NVIDIA packages).

### Key design decisions
- Uses `-c requirements/rocm.txt` constraint instead of `--torch-backend` (no ROCm backend in uv)
- Excludes torch/torchvision/torchaudio/triton and all nvidia-*/cuda-* packages from the lockfile output since they are managed by `rocm.txt` and `Dockerfile.rocm_base`

Will run a full build on ROCm CI to verify no new regressions are introduced.

cc @kenroche
author: AndreasKaratzas

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a `uv pip compile` workflow for ROCm test dependencies, aligning it with the existing CUDA setup. The changes include a new pre-commit hook, a verification step in the Dockerfile to ensure the correct PyTorch build is used, and regenerated lockfiles. My review focuses on improving the maintainability of the pre-commit configuration by simplifying the package exclusion list.
not a fan of the custom script
LGTM, it should be fairly easy to update the `--no-emit-package` rules and once proper ROCm wheels are distributed we should be able to use `--torch-backend` instead

title: [ROCM][Bugfix] Use correct stride in cp_mha_gather_cache_kernel for hybrid model (#37228)
body:
**Problem**
[_update_hybrid_attention_mamba_layout](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/e5b807607c8493155e6eccd665772d4c19b2114e/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py#L6359) uses as_strided_() to reorder KV blocks into a interleaved pattern. But cp_mha_gather_cache_kernel Triton kernel used hardcoded pointer arithmetic assuming contiguous memory. For hybrid models, this reads from wrong memory locations, producing garbage values and NaN in attention outputs.

Memory layout
Original Assumption: [K_all][V_all]
Hybrid model: [K_0][V_0][K_1][V_1] ... 

**Fix**
passed correct kernel stride to triton kernel.

TODO:
Currently, shuffle KV path also have the same issue. Need to fix it if it's needed for hybrid model

**Test**
Ran cmd in #35925, no corrupted responses observed anymore
author: jennyyyyzhen

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request correctly addresses an issue with handling non-contiguous KV cache tensors by passing and using explicit strides in the `cp_mha_gather_cache_kernel`. This is a good improvement for supporting hybrid models. However, the fix appears to be incomplete as it only applies to the 'NHD' cache format. I've left a critical comment pointing out that the 'SHUFFLE' cache format path still uses hardcoded strides, which will lead to incorrect behavior with non-contiguous tensors.
Looks good.

Use correct stride in cp_mha_gather_cache_kernel for hybrid model

title: [Bugfix][ROCm] Strip block_size before attention backend validation
body:
<!-- markdownlint-disable -->

## Purpose
The ROCm attention backend refactor (#35246) introduced validate_configuration calls that reject irregular block_size because it is not in the BlockSize type (1, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256). The CUDA platform avoids this by stripping block_size before validation. Apply the same fix to the ROCm platform.

## Test Plan
Can start Qwen3 Next correctly

This pull request addresses a bug on the ROCm platform where attention backend validation was failing for irregular `block_size` values. The fix, which mirrors the existing logic for the CUDA platform, involves stripping the `block_size` from the attention selector configuration before the validation step. This is a clean and targeted solution that resolves the issue, allowing models with non-standard block sizes to run correctly on ROCm. The change is well-contained and appears correct.

title: [CI] Fix conch kernel crash on 3D input by reshaping to 2D before GEMM
body:
When running quantized models (e.g. AWQ) through the transformers backend, the conch kernel crashes with:

ValueError: too many values to unpack (expected 2)
at `conch/ops/quantization/gemm.py:86`:
```python
m_dim, k_dim = x.shape
```

The transformers backend passes 3D tensors (batch, seq_len, hidden_dim) through linear layers, but mixed_precision_gemm expects a 2D input (M, K).

### Fix

Reshape x to 2D before calling mixed_precision_gemm and reshape the output back to match the original batch dimensions. This matches the existing pattern used by the machete kernel.

Test

- [x] `pytest tests/models/test_transformers.py::test_quantization -v`

cc @kenroche
author: AndreasKaratzas

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request modifies the `apply_weights` function in `vllm/model_executor/kernels/linear/mixed_precision/conch.py`. The input tensor `x` is now reshaped to a 2D tensor before being passed to `mixed_precision_gemm`, and the resulting output is subsequently reshaped back to its original multi-dimensional form. This change likely addresses compatibility requirements for the `mixed_precision_gemm` function while maintaining the expected output shape. There is no feedback to provide.

## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

title: [ROCm][CI] Fix AITER state leak in shared_fused_moe_routed_transform test
body:
The `use_rocm_aiter` monkeypatch only ran when `True`, so when a `False` test landed in the same shard after a `True` test, it inherited stale AITER state from `rocm_aiter_ops` class variables. This caused one module to use AITER and the other Triton, producing completely different results (max_diff=0.277). 

We always set env vars and call `refresh_env_variables()` on ROCm, not just when `use_rocm_aiter=True`.

- cleanup_dist_env_and_memory() now calls rocm_aiter_ops.refresh_env_variables() on ROCm, so every test that uses the standard cleanup gets AITER state reset for free.

## Test plan
- [x] 8/8 passed on MI325 gfx942 (4 AITER + 4 Triton, sequential in same process)

cc @kenroche
author: AndreasKaratzas

reviews.body:

This pull request modifies the `test_routed_input_transform_inside_vs_outside` function in `tests/kernels/moe/test_shared_fused_moe_routed_transform.py`. It simplifies a conditional check by removing the `use_rocm_aiter` flag, making the `monkeypatch.setenv` calls and import of `rocm_aiter_ops` dependent solely on `current_platform.is_rocm()`. There are no review comments to address.

title: [Mamba][APC] Add test case to compare apc outputs
body:
(Merge after https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/34798) 

* Added `test_same_mamba_output_apc_on_vs_off` to `test_hybrid.py` to check that output texts are identical whether prefix caching is enabled or not for the Mamba model.


https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/34798 fixes a bug in the `csrc/mamba/mamba_ssm/selective_scan_fwd.cu` where index calculation was incorrect with prefix caching enabled for mamba, hence leading to incorrect outputs. This PR builds upon https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/34798 to support the kernel fix and test the e2e correctness of mamba regardless of prefix caching settings. 

Outputs:

```
No prefix caching:  ['two what is one plus one what is one plus one what is one plus one the answer is two', 
                     'two what is one plus one what is one plus one what is one plus one the answer is two']
```
```
With prefix caching:
Before 34798: ['two two two the answer is two two two the answer is two two two the answer is two two', 
               'two the answer is two the answer is two the answer is two the answer is two the answer is']

After 34798:  ['two what is one plus one what is one plus one what is one plus one the answer is two', 
               'two what is one plus one what is one plus one what is one plus one the answer is two']
```


**NOTE**: I noticed that `test_hybrid.py` has `test_apc_common_prefix_same_batch` test which disables Multiprocessing for it's run. Now, there seems to be a known issue that vllm doesn't cleanup properly when multiprocessing is disabled. So, when `test_apc_common_prefix_same_batch` finishes, it doesn't cleanup the GPU memory for the next test in the file and rather rely on the pytest (python's GC) to finish for the cleanup. 
Hence, until a proper cleanup is setup in vllm for Multiprocessing=0 case, `test_apc_common_prefix_same_batch` should be set as the *last* test in this file.
author: divakar-amd

reviews.body:
## Code Review

The pull request introduces an end-to-end correctness test for the Mamba model to ensure that Automatic Prefix Caching (APC) does not alter the generated output. This is a valuable addition following a kernel-level fix for indexing issues. The test logic is sound and consistent with existing patterns in the repository, though it relies on a large model which may impact CI resource usage.
LGTM.

title: [Releases] [ROCm] Enable Nightly Docker Image and Wheel Releases for ROCm
body:
## Purpose

Address https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/36703

This PR enables Nightly Docker Image and Wheel Releases for ROCm

This PR can still stand on its own as it will build the base image and populate the cache if the base docker image is not found in the cache.

### User Experience Details

#### Docker

Following the CUDA Release pipeline, we are only keeping the docker image of latest 14 commits.

The docker image on dockerhub will be released with the following tag pattern:
- Base Docker Image (for traceability): 
  - `vllm/vllm-openai-rocm:base-nightly`
  - `vllm/vllm-openai-rocm:base-nightly-<commit>`
- vLLM OpenAI Docker Image:
  - `vllm/vllm-openai-rocm:nightly`
  - `vllm/vllm-openai-rocm:nightly-<commit>`

Example log:

```
[2026-03-23T17:42:32Z] + docker tag public.ecr.aws/q9t5s3a7/vllm-release-repo:1227c9527d573e09-rocm-base vllm/vllm-openai-rocm:base-nightly
[2026-03-23T17:42:32Z] + docker tag public.ecr.aws/q9t5s3a7/vllm-release-repo:1227c9527d573e09-rocm-base vllm/vllm-openai-rocm:base-nightly-57e207873b521dcbaba50b37153b0dd0b5883636
[2026-03-23T17:42:32Z] + docker tag public.ecr.aws/q9t5s3a7/vllm-release-repo:57e207873b521dcbaba50b37153b0dd0b5883636-rocm vllm/vllm-openai-rocm:nightly
[2026-03-23T17:42:32Z] + docker tag public.ecr.aws/q9t5s3a7/vllm-release-repo:57e207873b521dcbaba50b37153b0dd0b5883636-rocm vllm/vllm-openai-rocm:nightly-57e207873b521dcbaba50b37153b0dd0b5883636
```

#### Wheel

The ROCm wheels are located at the path:

- `https://wheels.vllm.ai/rocm/nightly/<variant>` now `https://wheels.vllm.ai/rocm/nightly/rocm700`
- `https://wheels.vllm.ai/rocm/36c72b2191380fa3809928f7b29880a499177457/rocm700/`

## Test plan

Trigger the pipeline

## Test results

Mock results https://buildkite.com/vllm/release-pipeline-shadow/builds/3264/steps/canvas


### Enhancement (Future plan)
Enhancement: ci-infra PR https://github.com/vllm-project/ci-infra/pull/297 to ensure the `Dockerfile.rocm_base` cache is always pre-populated. 

<details>

<summary> Archive </summary>

## Test Plan

Step 1: Trigger Ci-Infra PR https://github.com/vllm-project/ci-infra/pull/297 is triggered to populate the cache

Step 2: Trigger this PR
- Ensure that this Pipeline reuses the cached docker image and wheels from Step 1.

## Test Result

### Step 1: Trigger Ci-Infra PR https://github.com/vllm-project/ci-infra/pull/297 is triggered to populate the cache

https://buildkite.com/vllm/amd-ci/builds/6593/steps/canvas?sid=019cfb35-7dd4-4c22-8543-1b403a11356e&tab=output

The generated based docker image and wheels are

```
[2026-03-17T09:56:01Z] ROCm Base Image Build/Reuse
[2026-03-17T09:56:01Z]   Cache Key: b58dc988fa0856d2-9d3bce57
[2026-03-17T09:56:01Z]   ECR Cache Tag: public.ecr.aws/q9t5s3a7/vllm-release-repo:b58dc988fa0856d2-9d3bce57-rocm-base
[2026-03-17T09:56:01Z]   ECR Commit Tag: public.ecr.aws/q9t5s3a7/vllm-release-repo:0e4701ff0f6802aef64d449f7e8ab3c6599ca6e3-b58dc988fa0856d2-9d3bce57-rocm-base

[2026-03-17T09:56:04Z] Tagged public.ecr.aws/q9t5s3a7/vllm-release-repo:b58dc988fa0856d2-9d3bce57-rocm-base as public.ecr.aws/q9t5s3a7/vllm-release-repo:0e4701ff0f6802aef64d449f7e8ab3c6599ca6e3-b58dc988fa0856d2-9d3bce57-rocm-base in ECR (no pull required)
[2026-03-17T09:56:04Z] Base image ready: public.ecr.aws/q9t5s3a7/vllm-release-repo:0e4701ff0f6802aef64d449f7e8ab3c6599ca6e3-b58dc988fa0856d2-9d3bce57-rocm-base
```

#### Step 2: Trigger this PR

The results are in https://buildkite.com/vllm/release-pipeline-shadow/builds/3262/steps/canvas

Building docker vLLM image, the cached base docker image is downloaded and reused https://buildkite.com/vllm/release-pipeline-shadow/builds/3262/steps/canvas?sid=019d0e39-a357-44d8-a6f8-43ff0e15b1ac&tab=output
```
[2026-03-21T05:59:30Z] Pulling base Docker image from ECR: public.ecr.aws/q9t5s3a7/vllm-release-repo:b58dc988fa0856d2-9d3bce57-rocm-base
[2026-03-21T05:59:30Z] b58dc988fa0856d2-9d3bce57-rocm-base: Pulling from q9t5s3a7/vllm-release-repo
```


### Implementation Details

#### Challenges:

The ROCm releases depends on custom dependencies that are specified in `Dockerfile.rocm_base`. The build time of this docker file takes more than 3 hrs even after sccache enabled. The majority of the build time comes from `torch` and `amd-aiter` (if we choose to enable prebuilt again).

Current implementation reuses the caching logic from https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/32264 to cache the base docker image and dependency wheels (created from `Dockerfile.rocm_base`).

Ci-infra PR https://github.com/vllm-project/ci-infra/pull/297 to ensure the `Dockerfile.rocm_base` cache is always pre-populated.

</details>

author: tjtanaa

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces significant changes to the Buildkite release pipeline, primarily enabling nightly Docker image and wheel releases for ROCm. The changes involve a major refactoring of the ROCm build process, moving towards a more automated and cached approach using ECR for Docker images and S3 for wheels. Configuration is now centralized by extracting values directly from `Dockerfile.rocm_base`, which is a positive step for consistency. New scripts for ECR tag cleanup and Docker Hub nightly pushes have been added, enhancing pipeline management. However, there are several critical issues identified that prevent the intended functionality and require immediate attention.

Conditionally approving, pending the input from the infra folks regarding the potential additional load on the system

title: [Bugfix] Restore CUDA graph persistent buffers for FP8 FlashMLA decode
body:
## Purpose

Fix #33638: DeepSeek-V3.1 with `--kv-cache-dtype fp8` produces garbled output in v0.15.0.

The PR #32810 refactored the FlashMLA interface to use `FlashMLASchedMeta` objects. The non-FP8 path was adapted correctly - `FlashMLASchedMeta` manages its own internal buffers for CUDA graph safety. However, the FP8 path calls `get_mla_metadata_dense_fp8()` which allocates fresh raw tensors every call, and these were set directly on the `FlashMLASchedMeta` object without copying to persistent CUDA graph buffers.

Under the default O2 optimization level (`FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` CUDA graphs), the graph captures tensor addresses during recording. On replay, freshly-allocated tensors live at different addresses, so the kernel reads stale metadata from the originally-captured addresses, producing garbled output that starts normal then degenerates after ~50 tokens.

The persistent buffers (`cg_buf_tile_scheduler_metadata`, `cg_buf_num_splits`) were already allocated in `__init__` but were dead code - never used after the refactor. This PR restores the copy-into-persistent-buffer logic for the FP8 path in `_build_decode`, matching the v0.12.0 behavior and the pattern used by `FlashAttnMLAMetadataBuilder`.

## Test Plan

## Test Result
author: haosdent

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request correctly restores the use of persistent CUDA graph buffers for FP8 FlashMLA decode, fixing a bug that caused garbled output. The changes involve copying FP8 metadata into pre-allocated buffers to ensure tensor addresses remain constant across CUDA graph replays. The logic appears sound and addresses the issue described. I have one suggestion to improve robustness.
LGTM

commits.message:
[Bugfix] Restore CUDA graph persistent buffers for FP8 FlashMLA decode

Co-authored-by: Matthew Bonanni <mbonanni@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: haosdent <haosdent@gmail.com>

title: [ROCm][CI] Fix flaky GPTQ compile correctness test
body:
- Zero-initialize integer params (e.g. GPTQ `qweight`/`qzeros`) in `initialize_single_dummy_weight` on ROCm
- Fix `all_envs`/`all_args` length mismatch in compile correctness test
- Enable inductor comparison loop that was previously dead code

`initialize_single_dummy_weight` only initialized floating-point params, skipping integer tensors entirely. GPTQ's int32 parameters (`qweight`, `qzeros`) were left as `torch.empty()` leading to uninitialized GPU memory on ROCm.

The test's eager loop also had a bug: `all_envs.append({})` was called twice per mode (8 entries) while `all_args.append()` was called once (4 entries), then tripled with `all_args * 3` (12 entries). `zip()` truncated to 8 pairs, launching each mode twice in separate server processes. Different processes received different uninitialized int32 values, producing divergent outputs for identical configurations.

## Changes

- `vllm/model_executor/model_loader/weight_utils.py`: Add early return for non-float params, with `param.zero_()` on ROCm
- `tests/compile/fullgraph/test_basic_correctness.py`: Fix double `all_envs.append`, remove `all_args * 3`, enable inductor loop
  (was dead code due to empty `all_envs`)

## Test plan

- [x] `test_compile_correctness[test_setting1]` (GPTQ) on gfx942
- [x] Inductor + eager comparisons both run (no skips)
- [x] All 4 compile modes produce identical output in-process

cc @kenroche
author: AndreasKaratzas

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces changes to improve test correctness and weight initialization. In `tests/compile/fullgraph/test_basic_correctness.py`, it adjusts the `all_envs` list population for inductor and eager backends and corrects the argument list passed to `compare_all_settings`. In `vllm/model_executor/model_loader/weight_utils.py`, the `initialize_single_dummy_weight` function is refactored to handle non-floating point parameters by zero-initializing them on ROCm for reproducibility, and the existing floating-point and TPU initialization logic is reorganized. All review comments were filtered out as they primarily explained and validated the changes rather than pointing out new issues or improvement opportunities.

## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

[ROCm][CI] Fix flaky GPTQ compile correctness test

title: [ROCm][CI] Fix wvSplitKrc mock argument order in test_rocm_unquantized_gemm
body:
Fixes `test_rocm_unquantized_gemm_gfx950_wvsplitkrc_path` which was failing due to a copy-paste error in the mock setup.

`wvSplitK` and `wvSplitKrc` have reversed argument conventions in their C++ implementations:
- `wvSplitK(in_a=weight, in_b=x, ...)` is weight first
- `wvSplitKrc(in_a=x, in_b=weight, ...)` is activations first

The mock for `wvSplitKrc` was copied from the `wvSplitK` mock with the same `lambda w, x_view, ...` parameter order. Since `utils.py` calls `ops.wvSplitKrc(x, weight, cu_count, bias)` (activations first), the mock was receiving arguments in the wrong order and returning `weight @ x.T` (shape `256×16`) instead of `x @ weight.T` (shape `16x256`), causing `torch.allclose` to fail.

## Fix

- `tests/model_executor/layers/test_rocm_unquantized_gemm.py`: swap lambda parameter order in `wvsplitkrc_mock` from
  `lambda w, x_view, _, __` to `lambda x_view, w, _, __`

## Test

- [x] `pytest -s -v model_executor/layers/test_rocm_unquantized_gemm.py::test_rocm_unquantized_gemm_gfx950_wvsplitkrc_path`

cc @kenroche
author: AndreasKaratzas

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request corrects the argument order for the `wvSplitKrc` mock function in `tests/model_executor/layers/test_rocm_unquantized_gemm.py` to `x_view, w` instead of `w, x_view`. I have no feedback to provide.

## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.
LGTM

commits.message:
[ROCm][CI] Fix wvSplitKrc mock argument order in test_rocm_unquantized_gemm

title: [Bugfix][Minor] Fix potential NameError in mamba backend selector and misc typos
body:
## Summary

Fix a potential `NameError` bug in the mamba attention backend selector and several minor typos/grammar issues across the codebase.

### Bug fix

In `_cached_get_mamba_attn_backend` (`vllm/v1/attention/selector.py`), if `MAMBA_TYPE_TO_BACKEND_MAP[mamba_type]` raises a `KeyError`, the variable `backend_name` is never assigned. The `except` handler then references `backend_name` in the error message, which would raise a `NameError` instead of the intended `ValueError`. Fixed by using `mamba_type` (which is always bound) in the error message and listing valid mamba types.

### Typo/grammar fixes

| File | Fix |
|------|-----|
| `vllm/v1/attention/backends/utils.py` | "max a utility" -> "make a utility" |
| `vllm/v1/attention/backends/flex_attention.py` | `"Not yet my friend"` -> descriptive error message |
| `vllm/v1/attention/backends/rocm_aiter_fa.py` | "only support" -> "only supports" |
| `vllm/model_executor/models/kimi_k25.py` | "get" -> "got" in assertion message |
| `vllm/model_executor/models/transformers/pooling.py` | "the the" -> "the" (duplicated article) |

## Test plan

- All changes are to error messages, comments, and exception handlers - no functional logic is altered
- The `NameError` bug fix can be verified by inspecting the code: `backend_name` is only assigned inside the `try` block on the line that can raise `KeyError`
author: ChuanLi1101

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request addresses a potential `NameError` in the Mamba attention backend selector and includes several minor typo fixes. The main bug fix correctly handles cases where an invalid Mamba type is provided, preventing a crash and providing a more informative error message. The other changes, while minor, improve code readability and user-facing messages. The changes are well-implemented and I have no further suggestions.

 <sub>**Note:** Security Review did not run due to the size of the PR.</sub>
LGTM, thanks for doing this!

commits.message:
[Bugfix][Minor] Fix potential NameError in mamba backend selector and misc typos

Fix a bug in _cached_get_mamba_attn_backend where backend_name could be unbound when MAMBA_TYPE_TO_BACKEND_MAP lookup fails, causing NameError in the except handler instead of the intended ValueError.

Also fix several minor typos and grammar issues across attention backends and model files.

title: [Fix] Remove unused packing_position_embedding from PaddleOCRVL for better checkpoint compatibility
body:
## Summary

This PR removes the unused `packing_position_embedding` from the PaddleOCRVL model definition.

Previously, this embedding was instantiated in the model graph but was not used in the actual forward computation. As a result, it introduced extra parameter keys in the checkpoint/state dict, which could lead to unnecessary key mismatches when loading weights from different sources.

## Changes

- remove `packing_position_embedding = nn.Embedding(...)` from PaddleOCRVL
- align the model definition with the actual computation graph
- avoid generating or loading unused checkpoint keys related to `packing_position_embedding`

## Motivation

We want to keep weight loading backward compatible while supporting both:

- `model.safetensors` from Hugging Face repositories
- `safetensors` exported from the training pipeline

Removing this unused parameter makes checkpoint loading more robust and reduces redundant or unexpected keys during `load_weights`.

## Impact

- no change to the forward computation
- cleaner PaddleOCRVL model definition
- improved checkpoint compatibility across different weight sources
author: zhang-prog

reviews.body:
## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

## Code Review

This pull request refactors the `PaddleOCRVL` model by removing the `packing_position_embedding` and its associated logic from both the initialization and the `forward` method. The `forward` method now unconditionally applies position embedding interpolation based on `image_grid_thw`. A potential issue was identified where removing the conditional check for `image_grid_thw` could lead to a `TypeError` if `image_grid_thw` is `None`, as the original code had a fallback path. It is suggested to add an explicit check for `image_grid_thw` to prevent this runtime error and clarify the function's contract.

This reverts commit 678b3c99e82e1b1dd6cc95ff98c114393b788be4.

### Reason
The original PR caused **1 new CI failure** in nightly build [#58103](https://buildkite.com/vllm/ci/builds/58103):
- **Kernels (B200)**: CUDA coredump + `Fatal Python error: Aborted` in `test_flashinfer_cutedsl_moe_masked` (`tests/kernels/moe/test_cutedsl_moe.py`)

The crash occurs in `break_fp4_bytes` / `dequantize_nvfp4_to_dtype` during the masked MoE test on B200 GPUs, producing a GPU coredump with all SMs idle.

### Auto-generated
This revert PR was auto-generated by the CI failure analyzer.

This pull request refactors the FlashInfer CuteDSL MoE implementation by consolidating batched expert functionality into a single module and updating the `FlashInferCuteDSLExperts` class to directly use `flashinfer_cutedsl_grouped_gemm_nt_masked` for grouped GEMM operations, including explicit SILU and quantization. The `workspace_shapes` method was also revised for batched experts. A critical issue was identified where the `out` tensor in `flashinfer_cutedsl_moe_masked` is reassigned locally, preventing the caller from receiving the correct output. This needs to be addressed by copying the result back to the original `out` tensor.

title: [Hybrid] calling get_mamba_groups() once at MambaCopyBuffers.create()
body:
## Purpose

Now get_mamba_groups() is called only once during MambaCopyBuffers.create() and the result is reused in both preprocess_mamba() and postprocess_mamba() rather than being recomputed on every batch.

## Test Plan

python -m pytest tests/v1/worker/test_mamba_utils.py -v
author: fuscof-ibm

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request optimizes Mamba buffer handling by computing `get_mamba_groups()` only once during `MambaCopyBuffers.create()` and reusing the result. The change is logical and improves performance. However, I've identified a potential critical issue related to data consistency. The optimization introduces an implicit assumption that the `kv_cache_config` remains constant between the creation of `MambaCopyBuffers` and its usage in `preprocess_mamba` and `postprocess_mamba`. A mismatch could lead to serious errors. My review includes suggestions to add assertions to verify this assumption, making the implementation more robust.
LGTM!

title: [Cohere] Enable Cohere Transcribe
body:
Followup to https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35809
Turns on `Cohere Transcribe` model (official name) refered as `CohereAsrForConditionalGeneration` withing vLLM code. The official HF repo will be `CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026`. 

The PR also adds the model to test_transcription_api_correctness.py but doesnt enable it yet. Its adds additional value to this test because this model touches all the recent changes that was done to the variable length encoder inputs. Earlier, vLLM only had padded length encoder inputs which was suited for whisper but not for Cohere-Transcribe. 

That test calls a normalizer from the tokenizer but not all the models have a normalizer so we use the standard normalizer in the test for all the models in that test.

Cmd to run the model:
Install vLLM nightly and dependencies
```
uv pip install -U vllm --torch-backend=auto --extra-index-url https://wheels.vllm.ai/nightly
uv pip install vllm[audio]
uv pip install librosa
```

Start server
```
vllm serve CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026 --trust-remote-code
```

Send request
```
AUDIO_PATH=<your-audio-path>

curl -v -X POST http://localhost:8000/v1/audio/transcriptions \
-H "Authorization: Bearer $VLLM_API_KEY" \
-F "file=@$(realpath ${AUDIO_PATH})" \
-F "model=CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026"
```

Define helper function `call_hf_processor_mm_only` to replace `ProcessorMixin.__call__` in order to handle token inputs, since not all HF processors support empty text. This is so that we don't need to generate dummy text when calling HF processor on token inputs.

Note that this helper function isn't used whenever the MM processor overrides `_call_hf_processor`, since models that override this usually have additional postprocessing in the `AutoProcessor` class which are not covered by the helper function.
