title: [Bugfix] Fix DeepGemm E8M0 accuracy degradation for Qwen3.5 FP8 on Blackwell
body:
## Summary

Fixes #37804 — `Qwen/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-FP8` accuracy drops ~12pp on Blackwell (B200) when DeepGemm is active. DeepGemm's mandatory E8M0 (power-of-2 ceiling) scale format loses precision per FP8 block-quantized layer, compounding across all ~80 attention-projection and shared-expert FFN layers in Qwen3.5.


## Changes

- **Auto-disable DeepGemm for Qwen3.5 on Blackwell.** Adds a model-type exclusion list (`qwen3_5_text`, `qwen3_5_moe_text`) checked during config finalization on SM100+. When triggered, `use_deep_gemm` is set to `False` with a one-time warning. `VLLM_USE_DEEP_GEMM` env var still overrides.

- **Wire the override through the FP8 stack.** `Fp8Config` gains a `use_deep_gemm: bool | None` field that propagates through `Fp8LinearMethod` and `W8A8BlockFp8LinearOp`. UE8M0 weight pre-conversion at load time is skipped when DeepGemm is disabled, avoiding unnecessary precision loss.

- **Expand Blackwell GSM8K CI.** Adds `Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-FP8` and `Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-NVFP4` to the Blackwell test suite. Replaces the global `TOL = 0.08` constant with a per-config `tolerance` field.

## Accuracy Results (GSM8K, 1319 questions, 5-shot, B200 DP=2 EP, commit `2e67fa75`)

| Config | Run 1 | Run 2 | Run 3 | Mean |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DeepGemm ON — before fix | 0.6899 | 0.6967 | 0.6884 | **0.6917** |
| DeepGemm OFF (`VLLM_USE_DEEP_GEMM=0`) — before fix | 0.8097 | 0.8158 | 0.8112 | **0.8122** |
| **This PR** (auto-disabled for Qwen3.5) | **0.8006** | **0.8105** | **0.7976** | **0.8029** |

PS The PR includes changes from #37806 and #37889
author: vadiklyutiy

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces new Qwen3.5 model configurations for GSM8K evaluation, including specific accuracy thresholds and per-model tolerances. The evaluation script was updated to dynamically load these tolerances from the configuration files. A significant change involves implementing an auto-disable mechanism for DeepGemm quantization on Blackwell GPUs for certain model types (like Qwen3.5) to mitigate accuracy degradation, by introducing a `use_deep_gemm` flag in the FP8 configuration. A review comment suggests that the `self.use_ue8m0` condition in `input_quant_fp8.py` might be redundant and could be simplified.

title: Add `/v1/chat/completions/batch` endpoint for batched chat completions
body:
## Purpose
This PR adds a new `/v1/chat/completions/batch` endpoint to vLLM's OpenAI compatible API. The existing `/v1/chat/completions` endpoint only accepts a single conversation per request and remains unchanged. The new endpoint accepts messages as a `list[list[...]]`.

This is useful for applications that need to process multiple independent prompts with structured output in a single round trip, for example, extracting structured data from multiple documents simultaneously, reducing HTTP
  overhead and simplifying result handling since all outputs arrive in one response.
## Test Plan
I tested 4 cases:
0. Single conversation (non-batched): verified existing /v1/chat/completions behaviour is unchanged
 1. Batched plain text: sent 2 conversations in one request, verified 2 choices returned with correct index (0 and 1) and non-empty content
 2. Batched with json_schema: sent 2 conversations with a `JSON` schema constraint, verified each choice parses to valid JSON matching the schema                                                                                       
 3. Batched with regex constraint: sent 2 conversations with `(yes|no)` regex, verified each choice contains only yes or no
 4. Batched book summary extraction: sent 2 conversations extracting author, num_pages, short_summary, and long_summary from two books in a single request, verified both choices return correctly structured JSON
## Test Result
```
=== Example 1a: single conversation (standard endpoint) ===
  [0] The capital of Japan is Tokyo.

=== Example 1b: batched plain text (2 conversations) ===
  [0] The capital of France is Paris.
  [1] The capital of Japan is Tokyo.

=== Example 2: batch with regex constraint (yes|no) ===
  [0] yes
  [1] no

=== Example 3: batch with json_schema ===
  [0] {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30}
  [1] {'name': 'Bob', 'age': 25}

=== Example 4: batch book summaries ===
  [0] {'author': 'George Orwell', 'num_pages': 328, 'short_summary': 'A dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society ruled by Big Brother, following Winston Smith as he secretly rebels against the oppressive Party that surveils and controls every aspect of life.', 'long_summary': '1984 is a dystopian novel by George Orwell published in 1949. The story is set in a totalitarian society ruled by Big Brother, where the Party controls every aspect of life. Winston Smith, a low-ranking Party member, secretly rebels against the oppressive regime. The novel explores themes of surveillance, truth, and individuality in a society where the Party enforces its control through mind control and propaganda. '}
  [1] {'author': 'Douglas Adams', 'num_pages': 193, 'short_summary': 'A comedic science fiction novel following Arthur Dent, an ordinary Englishman who is whisked off Earth moments before it is demolished to make way for a hyperspace bypass, and his subsequent absurd adventures across the universe.', 'long_summary': "A humorous take on science fiction, the novel follows Arthur Dent, an ordinary British man, as he's unexpectedly transported to the alien planet of Trillian along with his human family. Here, they encounter a diverse cast of characters, including an eccentric alien named Zaphod Beeblebrox and his hapless wife Ford Prefect, who are all embroiled in a grand cosmic mystery. As the story unfolds, it becomes clear that the universe is far stranger and more chaotic than anything Arthur could have imagined, and his journey is one of self-discovery and unexpected friendships. "}
```
---

<summary> Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist </summary>

author: MatejRojec

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces support for batched chat completion requests, which is a valuable feature. The implementation is mostly solid, with new examples, tests, and protocol/serving logic changes. I've found two issues that should be addressed: one critical issue regarding the handling of the `n` parameter in batched requests which could lead to malformed responses, and a high-severity bug in the `echo=True` functionality for these new batched requests. After these are fixed, the PR should be in good shape.
Thanks for your patience! LGTM now

## Code Review

This pull request introduces a new `/v1/chat/completions/batch` endpoint for batched chat completions, complete with examples and tests. The implementation in `OpenAIServingChatBatch` handles concurrent processing of multiple conversations. Feedback highlights an inefficiency and potential bug in `OpenAIServingChatBatch` due to repeated request object creation and a type mismatch when calling `reasoning_parser.extract_reasoning`. Additionally, the `BatchChatCompletionRequest` model needs Pydantic validators to enforce its documented constraints, such as `use_beam_search=False` and `n=1`.

title: [Model] Add torch.compile support for InternVL vision encoder
body:
# [Model] Add torch.compile support for InternVL vision encoder

## Summary

<!-- markdownlint-disable -->
## Purpose

Add `torch.compile` support for the InternVL/InternViT vision encoder using the `@support_torch_compile` decorator pattern, following the established pattern from Qwen2.5-VL and other multimodal models.



**Changes:**
- Add `@support_torch_compile` decorator to `InternVisionEncoderLayer` with dynamic batch dimension support
- Wrap layer initialization with `set_model_tag` context manager for proper compile cache key generation
- Add `set_forward_context` wrapper in `extract_feature` for compilation context
- Fix `config/utils.py` to handle nested config objects (like `InternVLChatConfig`) that aren't directly JSON serializable by falling back to `to_dict()` when `to_json_string()` fails

## Test Plan

Tested with InternVL3-2B model using the vLLM benchmark suite:

```bash
# Terminal 1 - Start server with torch.compile enabled
vllm serve OpenGVLab/InternVL3-2B --trust-remote-code \
  --compilation-config='{"compile_mm_encoder":"true"}'

# Terminal 2 - Run benchmark
vllm bench serve \
  --backend openai-chat \
  --model OpenGVLab/InternVL3-2B \
  --endpoint /v1/chat/completions \
  --dataset-name hf \
  --dataset-path lmarena-ai/VisionArena-Chat \
  --hf-split train \
  --num-prompts 1000
```

## Test Result

Benchmark Results (InternVL3-2B on H100 80GB, 1000 prompts):


### Summary:

| Metric | Not Compiled | Compiled | Improvement |
|--------|-------------|----------|-------------|
| Benchmark duration (s) | 50.20 | 48.13 | **-4.1%** |
| Request throughput (req/s) | 19.92 | 20.78 | **+4.3%** |
| Output token throughput (tok/s) | 2011.66 | 2088.78 | **+3.8%** |
| Total token throughput (tok/s) | 3890.69 | 4048.71 | **+4.1%** |


### Benchmark Command

```bash
vllm bench serve \
  --backend openai-chat \
  --model OpenGVLab/InternVL3-2B \
  --endpoint /v1/chat/completions \
  --dataset-name hf \
  --dataset-path lmarena-ai/VisionArena-Chat \
  --hf-split train \
  --num-prompts 1000 \
  --request-rate inf
```

## Testing

- Verified encoder compilation via compile cache directory (`InternVisionEncoderLayer` directory created)
- No functional regressions — all requests succeeded with 0 failures
author: tianrengao

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request integrates `torch.compile` support for the `InternVisionEncoderLayer` by applying the `@support_torch_compile` decorator and utilizing the `set_model_tag` context manager during its initialization. Additionally, it incorporates `set_forward_context` within the `InternVL` model's `extract_feature` method, passing the `vllm_config`. A review comment suggests that the `is_encoder=True` argument in the `@support_torch_compile` decorator is redundant, as this configuration is already handled by the `set_model_tag` context manager, and recommends its removal for improved code clarity and maintainability.

commits.message:

title: [Models] Cohere Transcribe
body:
## Purpose
Add Cohere Transcribe to vLLM. 

It uses all the variable length encoder changes that were made in the last few months to vLLM scheduler to relax the constraint of having fixed length encoder inputs (https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/31058, https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/29268, https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/29278).

~~This model doen't have a HF impl yet so we added the HF processor, tokenizer and model config in vLLM similar to some other models in vLLM which dont have an HF impl.~~ Added to HF ckpt with trust remote code path.

The model weights will be soon available.


## Offline inference
```
VLLM_USE_V1=1 VLLM_WORKER_MULTIPROC_METHOD='spawn' python3 examples/offline_inference/audio_language.py -m cohere_asr
```

## Online inference
Terminal 1: start server
```
MODEL_ID=<model_path>
vllm serve $MODEL_ID --trust-remote-code
```

Terminal 2: run client
```
curl -v http://localhost:8000/v1/audio/transcriptions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $VLLM_API_KEY" \
  -F "file=@${AUDIO_PATH}" \
  -F "model=${MODEL_ID}" \
  -F 'language=en'
```

## Test 
WER test
```
time pytest -sv tests/entrypoints/openai/correctness/test_transcription_api_correctness.py::test_wer_correctness
```
author: ekagra-ranjan

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces support for the Cohere ASR model, a significant addition to vLLM's capabilities. The changes are extensive, including a new model implementation, a custom tokenizer, and a processor. Furthermore, the PR enhances the framework by adding a generic way to pass model-specific arguments (`vllm_xargs`) and introducing support for packed embeddings from multimodal encoders. While the overall implementation is solid, I've identified a few critical issues related to hardcoded device placement that must be addressed to ensure compatibility with different hardware backends. I've also noted a minor opportunity for code simplification in the input processing logic for better performance and clarity.

## Code Review

This pull request introduces support for the Cohere ASR model in vLLM. The changes are extensive, including a new model implementation, a custom processor since it's not on Hugging Face, and modifications to the data processing pipeline to accommodate its specific requirements. The introduction of the `skip_decoder_start_token` flag is a good example of a well-integrated change to support the new model's behavior. However, the PR includes hardcoded local file paths for the model in examples and test configurations, which is a critical issue that prevents the code from being runnable or testable by others. These paths should be replaced with placeholders or updated to use a downloadable model identifier once available.
LGTM, thanks for your patience!

title: [Bugfix] Fix Qwen3CoderToolParser anyOf/oneOf type resolution for nullable params
body:
## Purpose

Fix incorrect type resolution for `anyOf`/`oneOf` schemas, type-as-array patterns, and `$ref` schemas in `Qwen3CoderToolParser._convert_param_value`.

The previous fix (#36032) hardcoded `param_type = "object"` for all `anyOf` schemas. While this solved the double-encoding issue for object-typed `anyOf` params, it breaks nullable parameters with non-object types — they get routed through `json.loads` instead of their correct conversion branch (`int()`, `float()`, etc.).

This PR also subsumes the `$ref` handling proposed in #37652, integrated cleanly into the same `_resolve_param_type` helper.

### Root Cause

Pydantic v2 emits `anyOf` for every `Optional[T]` field:

```python
count: Optional[int] = 5
# becomes:
{"anyOf": [{"type": "integer"}, {"type": "null"}], "default": 5}
```

With the #36032 fix, this `anyOf` is resolved to `param_type = "object"`, so the value `"5"` goes through `json.loads("5")` — works by accident for integers, but for strings it would try to parse a plain string as JSON and fail or behave unexpectedly.

Similarly, Pydantic model inputs produce `$ref` schemas (e.g. `{"$ref": "#/$defs/ToolInput"}`) with no `"type"` field, which also fell through to `"string"`.

### Fix

Two new helper methods to keep type resolution logic clean and DRY:

**`_first_non_null_type(type_value)`** — static method that extracts the first non-null type from either a scalar (`"integer"`) or a type-as-array (`["integer", "null"]`). Reused in both top-level type resolution and anyOf variant scanning.

**`_resolve_param_type(param_def)`** — resolves the effective type string from a parameter definition. Handles four cases:
1. Direct `"type"` field (including type-as-array)
2. `anyOf`/`oneOf` variants (extracts first non-null type from variants)
3. `$ref` schemas (treated as `"object"` to trigger `json.loads`)
4. Fallback to `"string"`

This replaces the previous inline logic with deeply nested if/elif/else blocks, using early returns for clarity.

### Affected Cases

| Schema | #36032 (`"object"`) | This PR (actual type) |
|---|---|---|
| `anyOf[integer, null]` | wrong — routes to json.loads | `"integer"` → `int()` ✅ |
| `anyOf[string, null]` | wrong — routes to json.loads | `"string"` → as-is ✅ |
| `anyOf[array, null]` | partial — works but semantically wrong | `"array"` → `json.loads` ✅ |
| `anyOf[object, null]` | correct | `"object"` → `json.loads` ✅ |
| `anyOf[object, object]` (#36032 case) | correct | `"object"` → `json.loads` ✅ |
| `anyOf[string, integer, null]` | wrong — routes to json.loads | `"string"` (first non-null) ✅ |
| `{"type": ["integer", "null"]}` (top-level) | not handled | `"integer"` → `int()` ✅ |
| `anyOf[{"type": ["integer", "null"]}]` (nested) | not handled | `"integer"` → `int()` ✅ |
| `{"$ref": "#/$defs/Model"}` (#37652 case) | not handled | `"object"` → `json.loads` ✅ |

Also handles `oneOf` (used by FastAPI and other frameworks) and filters null types expressed as both `{"type": "null"}` (string) and `{"type": null}` (Python None, seen in some Pydantic outputs).

## Test Plan

Added `test_extract_tool_calls_anyof_type_conversion` covering 7 patterns:

```python
"anyof_int":       {"anyOf": [{"type": "integer"}, {"type": "null"}]}     → 5 (int) ✅
"anyof_str":       {"anyOf": [{"type": "string"}, {"type": "null"}]}      → "hello" (str) ✅
"anyof_array":     {"anyOf": [{"type": "array"}, {"type": "null"}]}       → ["a","b","c"] (list) ✅
"anyof_obj":       {"anyOf": [{"type": "object"}, {"type": "null"}]}      → {"key":"value"} (dict) ✅
"type_as_array":   {"type": ["integer", "null"]}                          → 42 (int) ✅
"multi_non_null":  {"anyOf": [{"type": "string"}, {"type": "integer"}, {"type": "null"}]}
                                                                          → "some text" (str, first non-null) ✅
"ref_param":       {"$ref": "#/$defs/ToolInput"}                          → {"city":"Paris"} (dict) ✅
```

Existing test suite passes with no regressions:
```
tests/tool_parsers/test_qwen3coder_tool_parser.py — 19 passed (+ 1 new = 20 total)
tests/tool_parsers/test_qwen3xml_tool_parser.py — 9 passed, 8 xfailed
```

## Follow-up

`Qwen3XMLToolParser._get_param_type` has the same issue — it uses `properties[param_name].get("type", "string")` which falls back to `"string"` for `anyOf` schemas. This PR intentionally scopes the fix to `Qwen3CoderToolParser` only. A follow-up PR for the XML parser will be submitted once this is merged.
author: AAISSJ

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request provides a crucial bugfix for tool parameter type resolution in `Qwen3CoderToolParser`, specifically for `anyOf`/`oneOf` schemas representing nullable types. The change correctly extracts the actual non-null type instead of hardcoding it as "object", which resolves issues with non-object nullable parameters. My review identifies a remaining edge case where the new logic does not handle a valid JSON Schema construct where the `type` field is an array (e.g., `{"type": ["integer", "null"]}`). I've provided a suggestion to make the type extraction more robust to cover this scenario.
thanks

[Bugfix] Fix Qwen3CoderToolParser anyOf/oneOf type resolution for nullable params

The previous fix (#36032) hardcoded param_type = "object" for all anyOf
schemas. This breaks nullable parameters with non-object types (e.g.
Optional[int], Optional[str], Optional[list]) because they get routed
through json.loads instead of their correct conversion branch (int(),
float(), etc.).

Pydantic v2 emits anyOf for every Optional[T] field:
  Optional[int] → {"anyOf": [{"type": "integer"}, {"type": "null"}]}

This fix extracts the first non-null type from anyOf/oneOf variants,
so each parameter hits its correct type conversion path.

Signed-off-by: AAISSJ <maze0717@g.skku.edu>

Handle type-as-array in anyOf/oneOf variants

Address review feedback: JSON Schema allows type to be an array
(e.g. {"type": ["integer", "null"]}). The previous code would
stringify the array, leading to unrecognized param_type. Now we
extract the first non-null element from type arrays as well.

Signed-off-by: AAISSJ <maze0717@g.skku.edu>

Add type-as-array handling at top level and anyOf test cases

- Handle {"type": ["integer", "null"]} at the top-level type field,

  anyOf[integer|null], anyOf[string|null], anyOf[array|null],
  anyOf[object|null], and top-level type-as-array

Signed-off-by: AAISSJ <maze0717@g.skku.edu>

Fix anyOf test to use qwen3_tool_parser fixture directly

The anyOf type conversion is implemented in Qwen3CoderToolParser,
not Qwen3XMLToolParser. Use the coder parser fixture directly
instead of the parametrized fixture (which only runs xml).

Refactor: extract _resolve_param_type and _first_non_null_type helpers

- Extract duplicated non-null type extraction into _first_non_null_type
  static method, reused for both top-level type-as-array and anyOf variants
- Extract _resolve_param_type to flatten nesting in _convert_param_value
- Add multi non-null type test case: anyOf[string, integer, null] verifies
  "first non-null type wins" policy

Signed-off-by: AAISSJ <maze0717@g.skku.edu>

Add $ref schema handling in _resolve_param_type

Parameters using $ref (e.g. Pydantic model inputs) have no "type" field.

extract_tool_calls_streaming) with anyOf nullable schemas to verify

The anyOf streaming test only covered string, integer, and boolean
params. Add a $ref parameter (filters) to verify that object type
resolution works correctly in the full streaming pipeline.

title: [Bugfix] Enable batch-invariant Triton matmul on all Ampere GPUs (SM 8x)
body:
## Purpose                                                                                                                                                                       
   
Enable batch-invariant Triton matmul on all Ampere GPUs (SM 80, 86, 87), fixing batch invariance on RTX 3090/3080/A6000/Jetson Orin.
                  
The Triton matmul overrides in `batch_invariant.py` were only registered for SM 80 (A100), SM 89 (4090), and SM 100 family (Blackwell). SM 86 (3090) was not matched by `is_device_capability(80)` (exact match), so it fell through to CUBLAS which picks different tiling/accumulation algorithms depending on batch size, breaking batch invariance in bfloat16.
                  
Fix: change `is_device_capability(80)` to `is_device_capability_family(80)`.

### How we found it                                                                                                                                                              

The determinism tests had 3 failures on RTX 3090. We isolated the source by testing each component individually (BS=1 vs BS=4):
  - `flash_attn_varlen_func` — batch-invariant ✓
  - `log_softmax` (Triton) — batch-invariant ✓
  - `torch.nn.functional.linear` (CUBLAS) — **not batch-invariant** ✗
  - `matmul_persistent` (Triton) — batch-invariant ✓

This confirmed CUBLAS matmul was the source, and traced why the Triton replacement wasn't active on SM 86 to the exact-match check.

Closes #38286                                                                                                                                                                    

## Test Plan

```bash
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/v1/determinism/ -v --timeout=1000

Test Result

Before fix (RTX 3090, SM 86): 97 passed, 3 failed
FAILED test_batch_invariance.py::test_decode_logprobs_match_prefill_logprobs[FLASH_ATTN]
FAILED test_online_batch_invariance.py::test_logprobs_bitwise_batch_invariance_bs1_vs_bsN[FLASH_ATTN]
FAILED test_online_batch_invariance.py::test_logprobs_bitwise_batch_invariance_bs1_vs_bsN[TRITON_ATTN]

After fix: 100 passed, 0 failed 
100 passed, 27 warnings in 318.41s 
                  
- Pre-commit checks pass
- AI assistance (Claude) was used for investigation and this PR
```
author: YM2132

reviews.body:
## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

## Code Review

This pull request updates the device capability check in batch_invariant.py to use is_device_capability_family(80) instead of a specific check for version 80. The reviewer pointed out that this change makes the subsequent check for capability 89 redundant, as the family check for 80 covers all SM 8.x architectures.

Thanks for the work!

We are very conservative about adding older gpus since we can't test it by ourselves, would you mind testing the following as well?

`deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2-Lite-Chat`
`Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking-2507-FP8`
Also enable the CI tests for you
LGTM, thanks for the work!

commits.message:
[Bugfix] Enable batch-invariant Triton matmul on all Ampere GPUs (SM 8x)

The batch-invariant matmul overrides in `batch_invariant.py` were only
registered for SM 80 (A100) and SM 89 (4090), missing SM 86 (3090/3080/
A6000) and SM 87 (Jetson Orin). This caused CUBLAS to be used instead of
the batch-invariant Triton kernel, and CUBLAS picks different algorithms
for different batch sizes, breaking batch invariance.

Fix: change `is_device_capability(80)` to `is_device_capability_family(80)`
to cover all Ampere variants.

Closes #38286

title: [Model] Add support for Cheers multimodal model
body:
## Purpose

This PR adds support for the [Cheers](https://huggingface.co/ai9stars/Cheers) multimodal model in vLLM. Cheers is a unified multimodal model (SiglipVision + Qwen2 LLM) that decouples patch-level details from semantic representations for both image understanding and generation ([arXiv:2603.12793](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.12793)).

Reference implementation: https://github.com/AI9Stars/Cheers

## Performance

### Multimodal Benchmarks

#### TextVQA

| Backend | Accuracy | Latency |
|--------|----------|--------------------|
| HF Transformers | 55.33% | 87.3s |
| vLLM | 55.13 | 18.8s |

#### MMStar

| Backend | Accuracy | Latency |
|--------|----------|--------------------|
| HF Transformers | 49.07% | 220.3s |
| vLLM | 48.93% | 75.2s |

## Test Plan

The following steps were used to validate the implementation:

### 1. Model Loading

```bash
vllm serve ai9stars/Cheers --trust-remote-code --dtype bfloat16 --max-model-len 4096
```

### 2. Offline Inference Test

```python
from vllm import LLM, SamplingParams
from PIL import Image
import requests
from io import BytesIO

if __name__ == '__main__':
    url = "https://picsum.photos/id/237/400/300"
    img = Image.open(BytesIO(requests.get(url).content)).convert("RGB")
    llm = LLM(
        model="ai9stars/Cheers",
        trust_remote_code=True,
        dtype="bfloat16",
        max_model_len=4096,
        gpu_memory_utilization=0.90,
    )

    prompt = (
        "<|im_start|>system\nYou are a helpful assistant.<|im_end|>\n"
        "<|im_start|>user\n"
        "<|image_pad|>Describe this image briefly.<|im_end|>\n"
        "<|im_start|>assistant\n"
    )

    outputs = llm.generate(
        [{"prompt": prompt, "multi_modal_data": {"image": img}}],
        SamplingParams(temperature=0.0, max_tokens=100),
    )

    for output in outputs:
        print(output.outputs[0].text)
```

### 3. OpenAI-Compatible API Test

```python
import base64, requests
from io import BytesIO

url = "https://picsum.photos/id/237/400/300"
img_bytes = requests.get(url).content
b64 = base64.b64encode(img_bytes).decode()

resp = requests.post(
    "http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions",
    json={
        "model": "ai9stars/Cheers",
        "messages": [
            {
                "role": "user",
                "content": [
                    {"type": "image_url", "image_url": {"url": f"data:image/png;base64,{b64}"}},
                    {"type": "text", "text": "Describe this image briefly."},
                ],
            }
        ],
        "max_tokens": 100,
        "temperature": 0,
    },
)
print(resp.json()["choices"][0]["message"]["content"])
```
## Test Result
✅ Model loads successfully without errors
✅ Inference runs correctly for both offline and API modes
✅ Output quality is consistent with HuggingFace Transformers inference
✅ No runtime crashes or tensor shape mismatches observed

Can you add this model to the Supported Models page, vision language examples and also `tests/models/registry.py`?

title: Don't compile vision encoder for Transformers backend
body:
vLLM doesn't compile the vision encoder for multimodal models, but when models are loaded with the Transformers modeling backend, the entire model is decorated for compilation.

This PR fixes this so that:

- for text only models, only the base model is decorated for compilation (i.e. the language modeling head is excluded)
- for vision models, the text model is always compiled and the vision model is only compiled when `-cc.compile-mm-encoder true` is passed

This will make using vision models with vLLM via the Transformers modeling backend behave as they would if they were loaded with the vLLM modeling backend.

---

Other useful PRs:

- https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/37345 - means that we don't need to also decorate the encoder with `set_model_tag`
- https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/37234 - fixes an issue dynamo has with `@can_return_tuple` from Transformers
author: hmellor

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request aims to prevent the vision encoder from being compiled when using the Transformers backend. The current implementation modifies the encoder class globally, which can lead to unintended side effects. My review suggests a safer approach by creating a subclass for the specific encoder instance to avoid global state modification.
Overall LGTM, but would like to let @ProExpertProg double check the compilation side as well.
This LGTM - thanks for the support and wiring through the dynamic dims

title: [Feature] KV cache per-token-head INT8/FP8 quantization
body:
Continue of PR: https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/36893 with changes requested by @mgoin
At comment:
https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/36893#issuecomment-4143255216

This PR adds per-token-head kv cache quantization to the Triton attention backend

reviews.body:
## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

## Code Review

This pull request introduces support for per-token KV cache quantization (INT8 and FP8) within the Triton attention backend. Key changes include new Triton kernels for reshaping and caching with dynamic per-token scales, updates to the unified attention kernels to handle dequantization, and modifications to the KV cache management logic to support packed scale bytes. Additionally, the PR adds comprehensive unit and end-to-end accuracy tests. Feedback was provided to align the reference quantization logic in the tests with the kernel's truncation behavior to enable stricter validation.
@tjtanaa could you help benchmark the triton backend to make sure we aren't introducing any regressions to the non-kv quant case?
Looks pretty solid to me now, thanks for all the iteration! Will look for others to sign off too before merge
Approved, but if there is time for some minor changes, it would be even better, otherwise we can see that in a follow-up PR.
LGTM thanks for the work on this PR @JartX !

fix: use kernel-level block dims for aux buffers and add total_memory_per_block

Auxiliary scale caches were allocated with logical (num_blocks, block_size)
dimensions, which causes out-of-bounds access in hybrid models (HMA) where
virtual block splitting produces more physical blocks at a smaller kernel
block size. Now uses kernel_block_sizes to compute the correct physical
layout.

Also adds KVCacheSpec.total_memory_per_block to encapsulate page_size_bytes
+ auxiliary_memory_per_block, replacing ad-hoc arithmetic in the block count
calculations so auxiliary memory cannot be accidentally omitted.

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

Replace separate auxiliary buffer allocations for per-token
quantization scales with packed storage inside the KV cache
block itself. Scales are stored directly after each K/V data
region using as_strided views into the same memory allocation.

Memory layout per block:
  [K data][K scales][V data][V scales]

This makes int8 per-token KV cache compatible with KV cache
transfer for disaggregated prefill/decode, since scales travel
with the data automatically.

- Remove AuxBufferSpec, auxiliary_buffer_specs, total_memory_per_block
- Include scale bytes in allocation_size_per_block via packed_scale_bytes_per_block
- Keep page_size_bytes data-only for unify_kv_cache_spec_page_size checks
- Replace _allocate_auxiliary_buffers with _create_packed_scale_views
- Use as_strided in _reshape_kv_cache_tensors for int8_per_token

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

fp8 per token

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

added test unit for fp8 per token

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

refactor: use flat contiguous tensor for packed KV cache

Replace as_strided data view (with gaps for scales) with a flat
uint8 tensor shaped (num_blocks, 2, kv_half_bytes) that covers
data + scales contiguously. Typed data and scale views are created
once at init via as_strided into the same storage.

This ensures KV cache transfers move everything with no ambiguity,
matching the fp8_ds_mla pattern where one tensor covers all bytes.

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

fix load fp8 per token

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

refactor: move packed layout logic into attention backend

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

fix: keep page_size_bytes data-only, add scale_bytes_per_block to AttentionSpec

page_size_bytes must exclude scale bytes to preserve divisibility
for unify_kv_cache_spec_page_size. Scale bytes are exposed via
AttentionSpec.scale_bytes_per_block and added at allocation sites
using getattr fallback.

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

fix load to(Q.dtype)

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

refactor: move flat KV shape into get_kv_cache_shape, zero model runner changes

Move per-token quant flat shape logic into TritonAttentionBackend.
get_kv_cache_shape() now returns (num_blocks, 2, kv_half) for
per-token dtypes. The model runner uses the generic reshape path
for all backends and dtypes with no special casing.

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

fix: pass cache_dtype_str to get_kv_cache_stride_order

The flat 3D shape for per-token quant has 3 dims but the stride
order returned 5. Pass cache_dtype_str so the backend returns the
correct stride order length. Add **kwargs to the base interface
for forward-compatibility.

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

fix: handle stride order length mismatch for flat 3D KV shape

When get_kv_cache_shape returns a 3D flat shape for per-token quant,
get_kv_cache_stride_order still returns 5 elements. Use a length
check fallback instead of exception handling. No changes to other
backends or the base interface.

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

restore comments

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

fix: restore allocation size assert and original comments in model runner

Restore the comments explaining stride order permutation logic
that were accidentally dropped. Add back a size assert using
page_size_bytes + scale_bytes_per_block to validate the raw
tensor has enough space for num_blocks.

Signed-off-by: JartX <sagformas@epdcenter.es>

restore position of comment

optimize memory band usage

optimize performance

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a fused Triton kernel to optimize the post-convolution operations in the Gated Delta Net (GDN) layer of the Qwen3.5 model. The new kernel combines split, rearrange, contiguous, L2 normalization, and gating operations. The changes include the fused kernel implementation, corresponding correctness tests against a reference, and integration into the `qwen3_next.py` model file. The integration logic correctly handles different backends and avoids redundant L2 normalization. The tests cover various configurations and edge cases.

## Code Review

This pull request optimizes the GDN prefill path by introducing a fused Triton kernel, `fused_post_conv_prep`, which combines split, rearrange, L2 normalization, and gating operations into a single pass. The kernel is integrated into the `GDNLinearAttention` layer, replacing several discrete operations to improve efficiency. Comprehensive tests are included to verify correctness across various configurations. Review feedback suggests improving the numerical stability of the softplus implementation within the Triton kernel and adding a descriptive error message to a new assertion for better debuggability.
LGTM

title: [ROCm] Enable VLLM triton FP8 moe for gfx1201, tuned for Qwen3-30B-A3B-FP8 tp=2 and Qwen/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-FP8 tp=2
body:
## Purpose
Enable Triton FP8 MoE for RDNA4 (`gfx12xx`) in vLLM so FP8 MoE models can run on ROCm with these GPUs.

This resolves https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/36105, where FP8 MoE model startup failed with `NotImplementedError: No FP8 MoE backend supports the deployment configuration`.

This PR also includes tuned Triton MoE performance improvements for:
- `Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507-FP8`
- `Qwen/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-FP8`

## Test Plan
benchmark Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507-FP8 and Qwen/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-FP8 with triton moe tuned on 2 Radeon PRO 9700

```bash
# Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507-FP8
VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=0 vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507-FP8 -tp 2 --enable-expert-parallel --gpu-memory-utilization 0.95 --max-model-len 65536

# Qwen/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-FP8
Qwen/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-FP8 -tp 2 --enable-expert-parallel --gpu-memory-utilization 0.95 --max-model-len 65536
```

title: [Bugfix] Use dedicated MM processor cache in /tokenize to prevent sender-cache pollution
body:
## Purpose

Fix `/v1/chat/completions` failing after `/tokenize` is called with the same multimodal input (e.g. an image).

`/tokenize` runs multimodal preprocessing but does not send anything to the engine core. This leaves stale sender-cache entries that cause subsequent `/v1/chat/completions` requests to fail.

Fixes https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/38543

## Approach

- Create a second `BaseMultiModalProcessor` with its own `processor_only_cache` (via `mm_registry.processor_only_cache_from_config`) dedicated to the tokenize endpoint. Full isolation — no shared mutable state with the main sender cache.
- Thread `skip_mm_cache=True` through the render call chain so tokenize requests are routed to the dedicated processor.

## Test Plan

```bash
python -m pytest tests/entrypoints/serve/tokenize/test_tokenize_then_chat_vlm.py -v
```
author: sergey-zinchenko

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request addresses a bug where the /tokenize endpoint incorrectly cached multimodal features, causing subsequent /v1/chat/completions requests with the same data to fail. The implementation introduces a ContextVar called skip_mm_processor_cache to bypass the multimodal processor cache during tokenization, preventing the SenderCache from being polluted with entries that won't be transmitted. A regression test was also added to ensure that calling /tokenize before chat completions with multimodal data works correctly. I have no feedback to provide.

## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

commits.message:
[Bugfix] Add regression tests for multimodal tokenization and chat completion; evict unsent cache entries

Signed-off-by: Sergey Zinchenko <sergey.zinchenko.rnd@gmail.com>

[Bugfix] Bypass multimodal processor cache in tokenize endpoint to prevent SenderCache pollution

title: [Perf] DSV3.2 Indexer Fused Weights Projection
body:
## Purpose

Fuse the WK and Weights_Proj projections in the DSV3.2 Indexer. This is an alternative optimization to https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35968, which overlaps the projections instead of fusing them. Doing the fusion provides a greater speedup:

Benchmark timings for DSV3.2 NVFP4 on 8xB200 (TP8, No Specdec)

### BS128 8k/1k
```
Fused WK+WeightsProj (Decode):  21.6 ms
Baseline             (Decode):  22.3 ms
```

### BS1 8k/1k
```
Fused WK+WeightsProj (Decode):  9.5 ms
Overlap              (Decode):  9.8  ms
Baseline             (Decode):  10.2  ms

Fused WK+WeightsProj (TTFT):  339 ms
Overlap              (TTFT):  340 ms
Baseline             (TTFT):  341 ms
```

## Testing

GSM8k shows a slight decrease.

PR (2 runs):
```
|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|     5|exact_match|↑  |0.9500|±  | 0.006|
|     |       |strict-match    |     5|exact_match|↑  |0.9492|±  | 0.006|

|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|     5|exact_match|↑  |0.9553|±  |0.0057|
|     |       |strict-match    |     5|exact_match|↑  |0.9530|±  |0.0058|
```

Main (2 runs):
```
|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|     5|exact_match|↑  |0.9538|±  |0.0058|
|     |       |strict-match    |     5|exact_match|↑  |0.9545|±  |0.0057|

|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|     5|exact_match|↑  |0.9462|±  |0.0062|
|     |       |strict-match    |     5|exact_match|↑  |0.9462|±  |0.0062|
```
author: benchislett

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request fuses the "wk" and "weights_proj" layers into a single "MergedColumnParallelLinear" named "wk_weights_proj" within the DeepSeek-V2 and MTP models to optimize performance. Several critical issues were identified: forcing "quant_config=None" may lead to loading errors with quantized checkpoints, the tensor slicing in the forward pass assumes a 2D input which could fail with 3D tensors (a flattening suggestion was provided), and the weight loading logic is susceptible to name corruption and potential crashes due to substring matching and missing guard conditions.
LGTM. Projections are tiny, bandwidth-bound, and same precision. Fusion wins :)

fused wk + weights_proj

Merge branch 'main' into perf/deepseek-fused-wk

title: [Bugfix] Preserve original ImportError in gRPC server entrypoint
body:
## Purpose

Fix misleading error message when `smg-grpc-servicer` is installed but has a broken internal import (e.g. version mismatch between servicer and vllm, or a missing transitive dependency like grpcio).

Currently `from None` suppresses the original traceback, so the user only sees:
```
ImportError: smg-grpc-servicer is required for gRPC mode.
Install it with: pip install vllm[grpc]
```

This is misleading when the package IS installed but an internal import fails — the real error is hidden.

## Test Plan

Manual verification: introduce a broken import inside `smg_grpc_servicer` and confirm the chained traceback is now visible.

## Test Result

After this change, the original `ImportError` is chained and visible:
```
ImportError: <real error details>

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

ImportError: gRPC mode requires smg-grpc-servicer.
If not installed, run: pip install vllm[grpc].
If already installed, there may be a broken import due to a
version mismatch — see the chained exception above for details.
```

---

- [x] The purpose of the PR: preserve original traceback for gRPC import errors
- [x] The test plan: manual verification of error output
- [x] The test results: chained exception now visible
author: CatherineSue

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request improves the error handling for gRPC-related imports in vllm/entrypoints/grpc_server.py. The updated logic now captures the original ImportError and provides a more descriptive error message to help users diagnose potential version mismatches or installation issues. I have no feedback to provide as there are no review comments.

commits.message:
[Bugfix] Preserve original ImportError in gRPC server entrypoint

Change `from None` to `from e` so the original traceback is visible
when smg-grpc-servicer is installed but has a broken import (e.g.
version mismatch, moved function). Previously the real error was
swallowed and replaced with a generic "install the package" message.

title: [Feat][Executor] Introduce RayExecutorV2
body:
## Purpose
- Implement RayExecutorV2, a new Ray-based distributed executor that uses `MessageQueue` (shared memory + TCP fallback) for the control plane instead of Ray compiled graphs. It reuses `MultiprocExecutor`'s MQ-based RPC and NCCL data plane while spawning workers as Ray actors into placement group bundles.
- Workers on the same node as the driver communicate via shared memory; cross-node workers automatically fall back to ZMQ TCP transport. Bundle assignments are sorted driver-node-first to ensure rank 0 is co-located with the executor.                                                                                                                                                                      
- Add `VLLM_USE_RAY_V2_EXECUTOR_BACKEND` env var feature flag (default off) to opt into the new executor when `distributed_executor_backend="ray"`. Enable async scheduling support for the new backend.

For more details, please refer to RFC: https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/35848.

EEP support is out-of-scope for this PR and is tracked here: https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/38164.

## Test Plan
### Unit tests
- `pytest tests/distributed/test_ray_v2_executor.py`: executor init, TP/PP combos, placement groups, RPC, worker death, shutdown                                                                         
- `pytest tests/utils_/test_ray_utils.py`: bundle sorting logic  
- Validate cross-node TCP path for `MessageQueue` with `test_mq_tcp_multinode.py`

### Integration tests
- `pytest tests/distributed/test_ray_v2_executor_e2e.py`: Creates Ray actors which initialize AsyncLLMEngine internally and verify that they can serve requests.
- `pytest tests/distributed/test_pipeline_parallel.py -k "ray"`: PP correctness with the new backend                                                                                                     
- `pytest tests/basic_correctness/test_basic_correctness.py -k "ray"`: basic correctness  

## Test Result
### Benchmark results (Qwen/Qwen3-8B on L4)
Server:
```

vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3-8B --tensor-parallel-size 4 --distributed-executor-backend mp --port 8000

# Ray V2 backend

```

title: [Refactor] Simplify FutureWrapper in MultiprocExecutor
body:
## Purpose

Refactor `FutureWrapper` in `MultiprocExecutor` to simplify the response-fetching pattern.

- **`vllm/v1/executor/multiproc_executor.py`**:
  - `FutureWrapper.__init__` now accepts the `get_response` callable directly and appends itself to the futures queue, instead of the caller managing `(future, callable)` tuples.
  - `collective_rpc` always creates a `FutureWrapper` and calls `future.result()` for the blocking path, removing the separate drain loop and the duplicated `aggregate(get_response())` call.

## Test Plan

```bash
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/distributed/test_multiproc_executor.py::test_multiproc_executor_multi_node -v
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/v1/executor/test_executor.py -v
```

```
vllm bench latency --model meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --batch-size 256 --input-len 2048 --output-len 2048 -tp 4 --num-iters-warmup 1 --num-iters 5
```

## Test Result

Before:
```
Avg latency: 28.613585791923107 seconds
10% percentile latency: 28.591742496564983 seconds
```

- [ ] The test plan, such as providing test command.

- [ ] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model.
- [ ] (Optional) Release notes update. If your change is user facing, please update the release notes draft in the [Google Doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YyVqrgX4gHTtrstbq8oWUImOyPCKSGnJ7xtTpmXzlRs/edit?tab=t.0).
</details>
author: yzong-rh

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request refactors the FutureWrapper class to encapsulate response retrieval and simplifies future management within the multiprocess executor. The review identified critical issues in the result() and wait_for_response() methods, where the lack of state checks could lead to incorrect queue draining or the consumption of responses intended for other RPC calls. Suggestions were provided to ensure these methods correctly check if a future is already completed before proceeding.

Note that the [.done() shortcut](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/b6e636c12c28c9ae83e22c65b4fca64a5571483b/vllm/v1/engine/core.py#L481) in enigne core is almost always false for MultiprocExecutor.

This is because `FutureWrapper` is cooperative: `set_result` is only called inside `wait_for_response()`, which is only invoked by an explicit `.result()` call on the same thread — there is no background thread resolving futures.
Thanks @yzong-rh, this looks reasonable to me, given we care most about the non-blocking case now anyhow.

commits.message:
[Refactor] Simplify FutureWrapper in MultiprocExecutor

Promote the get_response callable into a FutureWrapper field,
eliminating the deque[tuple[FutureWrapper, Callable]] pattern.
Both blocking and non-blocking paths in collective_rpc now go
through futures_queue and future.result(), removing the separate
drain loop and the duplicated aggregate(get_response()) call.

Co-authored-by: Claude
Signed-off-by: Yifan Zong <yzong@redhat.com>

Signed-off-by: Yifan <yzong@redhat.com>

Make result() idempotent

Signed-off-by: Yifan <yzong@redhat.com>

Addr comments

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a fused CUDA kernel for SiLU, multiplication, and block-wise FP8 quantization, along with corresponding benchmarks, tests, and integration into the `torch.compile` fusion passes. The new kernel shows significant performance improvements in the provided benchmarks.

My review has identified a couple of important issues:
1.  A critical issue in the `torch.compile` fusion pass where the pattern for the new fused kernel is hardcoded for a single `group_size`, which will prevent fusion for other supported sizes.
2.  A high-severity issue in the CUDA kernel implementation regarding a hardcoded shared memory size, which makes the code brittle and prone to future bugs.

Addressing these points will improve the correctness and maintainability of the new feature. The rest of the changes, including the tests and benchmark code, look solid.

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 2 potential issues.

<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with Cloud Agents, enable Autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>



_Comment `@cursor review` or `bugbot run` to trigger another review on this PR_

Do we have any E2E model cases we can add this to? Perhaps a non-moe Qwen? We should add it to E2E tests.

Can you also try to manually invoke the fused kernel inside an moe block quant method and see if we can get speedup for moe models? Sadly the compile pass won't work on those yet because the whole layer is wrapped in a custom op
Nice and clean, good work! Please resolve the merge conflicts

title: [MoE Refactor] Mxfp4 oracle rebased
body:
## Purpose
Rebased and improve version of #34983
Ongoing MXFP4 MoE refactor

- Refactor MXFP4 MoE from a monolithic 1299-line `Mxfp4MoEMethod` class to the oracle pattern used by FP8 and NvFP4
  - Create `oracle/mxfp4.py` with backend selection, weight conversion, quant config, and kernel assembly
  - Create `TrtLlmMxfp4ExpertsMonolithic` wrapping `trtllm_fp4_block_scale_moe()` (both BF16 and MXFP8 input modes)
  - Create `OAITritonMxfp4ExpertsMonolithic` wrapping `triton_kernel_moe_forward()`
  - Implement `_supports_*` methods on `TrtLlmGenExperts` and `BaseOAITritonExperts` so they work with the oracle
  - Consolidate monolithic/modular into single backend enum values following FP8 pattern (`backend_to_kernel_cls` returns
  `[Monolithic, Modular]` list, `is_supported_config` selects naturally)
  - Move MXFP4-specific hidden_size rounding from `layer.py` into oracle
  - Simplify `mxfp4.py` from 1299 to ~430 lines — all inline kernel code removed

  ## Test plan
  GPQA eval on gpt-oss-120b (target ~65.3%):

  | Backend | default | TP=2 | DP=2 EP |
  |---------|---------|------|---------|
  | FLASHINFER TRTLLM MXFP4+BF16 (monolithic/modular) | 65.53% | 65.53% | 66.35% |
  | FLASHINFER TRTLLM MXFP4+MXFP8 (monolithic/modular) | 64.39% | 65.21% | 66.09% |
  | FLASHINFER CUTLASS MXFP4+MXFP8 (modular) | 65.66% | 64.39% | fail on main |
  | TRITON monolithic/modular | 65.66% | 66.41% |  See Note |

Triton kernel for DEP=2 will hit this [error](https://github.com/openai/harmony/issues/80) but both model generate reasonable outputs. 
 
DP/EP for CUTLASS mxfp8 are failing due to kernel reason. Since it fails on main, we can defer to later PRs.


---

<summary> Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist </summary>

- [x] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)".
- [x] The test plan, such as providing test command.
- [x] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results
- [ ] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model.
- [ ] (Optional) Release notes update. If your change is user facing, please update the release notes draft in the [Google Doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YyVqrgX4gHTtrstbq8oWUImOyPCKSGnJ7xtTpmXzlRs/edit?tab=t.0).
</details>
author: zyongye

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request is a significant and well-executed refactoring of the MXFP4 MoE implementation. Moving the backend-specific logic into an oracle pattern greatly improves modularity and maintainability, as evidenced by the substantial reduction in code complexity within `mxfp4.py`. The new structure is much cleaner and easier to follow. I have one critical comment regarding proper object initialization in a base class, which could lead to subtle bugs.

## Code Review

This pull request refactors the MXFP4 Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) backend selection and implementation by introducing a dedicated `mxfp4` oracle. Key changes include centralizing backend selection logic, defining new modular and monolithic TRTLLM MXFP4 expert classes, and updating Triton and Marlin implementations to support MXFP4 quantization. The `Mxfp4MoEMethod` now uses the new oracle for backend selection, dimension rounding, and kernel setup, removing previous ad-hoc logic. A new test file was added to verify the oracle's selection logic. A review comment highlights a potential bug where the `routing_method_type` in the modular TRTLLM MXFP4 expert is hardcoded instead of using the configured value, which should be corrected for consistency and correctness.

Thanks for the contribution! LGTM if @mgoin or @robertgshaw2-redhat are ok as well.

~~Maybe add gpt-oss to `tests/evals/gsm8k/configs/moe-refactor`?~~ Tests already exist in `tests/evals/gpt_oss/configs`
LGTM overall, just comments on a few things that seem to be missing

commits.message:
[Refactor] Move MXFP4 MoE quantization to oracle pattern with proper Expert classes

Refactor MXFP4 MoE from a monolithic 1299-line class to the oracle
pattern used by FP8 and NvFP4. This separates backend selection,
weight conversion, and kernel assembly into oracle/mxfp4.py, and
wraps monolithic kernel calls into proper FusedMoEExpertsMonolithic
classes.

Key changes:
- New oracle/mxfp4.py: Mxfp4MoeBackend enum, select_mxfp4_moe_backend(),
  convert_to_mxfp4_moe_kernel_format(), make_mxfp4_moe_quant_config(),
  make_mxfp4_moe_kernel(), mxfp4_round_up_hidden_size_and_intermediate_size()
- New experts/trtllm_mxfp4_moe.py: TrtLlmMxfp4ExpertsMonolithic wrapping
  trtllm_fp4_block_scale_moe() for both BF16 and MXFP8 input modes
- New OAITritonMxfp4ExpertsMonolithic in gpt_oss_triton_kernels_moe.py
  wrapping triton_kernel_moe_forward()
- Implemented _supports_* on TrtLlmGenExperts and BaseOAITritonExperts
- Removed max_capture_size param from TrtLlmGenExperts (computed internally)
- Moved MXFP4-specific hidden_size rounding from layer.py to oracle
- Simplified mxfp4.py to thin orchestrator (1299 -> ~456 lines)
- Updated quark_moe.py imports to use oracle

GPQA eval results on gpt-oss-120b (target ~65.3%):
- MXFP4+BF16 monolithic (default): 65.53%
- MXFP4+BF16 monolithic (TP=2):    65.53%
- MXFP4+MXFP8 monolithic (default): 64.39%
- MXFP4+MXFP8 monolithic (TP=2):    65.21%
- CUTLASS MXFP4+MXFP8 modular (default): 65.66%
- CUTLASS MXFP4+MXFP8 modular (TP=2):    64.39%
- DP=2 EP (all backends): fail on main (pre-existing assertion)
- Triton monolithic: fail on main (pre-existing shape assertion on SM100)

CK (ROCm) backend refactor deferred to follow-up PR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Signed-off-by: Yongye Zhu <zyy1102000@gmail.com>

[Refactor] Consolidate MXFP4 monolithic/modular backend enums

Follow the FP8 oracle pattern: use a single backend enum value
(e.g. FLASHINFER_TRTLLM_MXFP4_BF16) for both monolithic and modular
expert classes. backend_to_kernel_cls() returns a list
[Monolithic, Modular] and _return_or_raise() iterates it, letting
is_supported_config() naturally select the right class based on the
deployment configuration (EP → modular, no EP → monolithic).

Removes: FLASHINFER_TRTLLM_MXFP4_BF16_MONOLITHIC,
FLASHINFER_TRTLLM_MXFP4_MXFP8_MONOLITHIC, TRITON_MONOLITHIC enums
and MONOLITHIC_BACKENDS tuple. is_monolithic is now determined by
self.moe_mk.is_monolithic (from the selected expert class).

title: [Bugfix] Restrict TRTLLM attention to SM100, fixing GB300 (SM103) hang
body:
## Summary

All models hang indefinitely on GB300 (SM103, CC 10.3) during inference with large batch sizes. The GPU shows 99% SM utilization and 0% memory bandwidth. This is a regression introduced by the FlashInfer 0.6.6 to 0.6.7 upgrade, where TRTLLM attention kernels are no longer forward-compatible with SM103.

GB200 (SM100) is not affected.

Related FlashInfer issue: [flashinfer-ai/flashinfer#2939](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/issues/2939) (fix ongoing on the FlashInfer side)

Fixes #38729

## Change

Restrict `supports_trtllm_attention()` to exact SM100 (CC 10.0) instead of the CC 10.x family. SM103 falls back to the FlashInfer default attention backend, which works correctly.

## Test plan

- [x] Qwen3-8B-FP8 throughput benchmark on GB300 (SM103): was hang, now passes
- [x] Qwen2.5-7B BF16 throughput benchmark on GB300 (SM103): was hang, now passes
- [x] Verify no regression on GB200/B200 (SM100): should still use TRTLLM attention
author: stecasta

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request restricts TRTLLM attention support specifically to SM100 (Compute Capability 10.0) to avoid hangs reported on SM103. A critical bug was identified in the implementation where the device capability check was incorrectly called with two arguments, which would fail to detect SM100 devices and cause a regression for intended users.

commits.message:
[Bugfix] Restrict TRTLLM attention to SM100, fixing GB300 (SM103) hang

TRTLLM attention kernels hang on GB300 (SM103, CC 10.3) with
FlashInfer >= 0.6.7. All models are affected (FP8, FP4, BF16) at
large batch sizes, while small correctness tests pass.

Restrict supports_trtllm_attention() to exact SM100 (CC 10.0) instead
of the CC 10.x family. SM103 falls back to the FlashInfer default
attention backend, which works correctly.

Fixes #38729
See also: https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/issues/2939

Co-authored-by: Claude
Signed-off-by: Stefano Castagnetta <scastagnetta@nvidia.com>

Fix is_device_capability call to use integer format

Use is_device_capability(100) instead of is_device_capability(10, 0)
to match the existing codebase convention. The two-arg form would be
interpreted as capability=10, device_id=0, checking for SM1.0 instead
of SM10.0.

Co-authored-by: Claude
Signed-off-by: Stefano Castagnetta <scastagnetta@nvidia.com>

Fix ruff formatting: remove unnecessary parentheses

Co-authored-by: Claude
Signed-off-by: Stefano Castagnetta <scastagnetta@nvidia.com>
Merge branch 'main' into fix-gb300-trtllm-hang

docs: handle is_device_capability() in attention backend docs generator

The TRTLLM attention check was changed from is_device_capability_family(100)
to is_device_capability(100). Update the docs generator to handle both
patterns, and regenerate the attention backends doc.

title: [Kernel] Add MXFP8 to Marlin GEMM/MoE and refactor Mxfp8LinearOp
body:
<!-- markdownlint-disable -->

## Purpose

The Marlin kernel already supports FP8 (per-channel/group scales) and MXFP4 (per-32-element e8m0 scales). MXFP8 is a natural combination: FP8 weights (like existing FP8 Marlin) with e8m0 microscaling block scales (like existing MXFP4 Marlin). We just have to wire the kernel building blocks together.

This PR also consolidates gemm kernel backend specific logic more into the Mxfp8LinearOp class for modelopt.py and mxfp8.py

## Test Plan

Existing online quant test for mxfp8 will now run on L4 in CI `tests/models/quantization/test_mxfp8.py`

## Test Result

```
vllm serve mgoin/Qwen3-0.6B-MXFP8
vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B --quantization mxfp8
vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B --quantization mxfp8
```

This pull request adds support for MXFP8 quantization in the Marlin kernel, providing a faster alternative to the existing emulation path. The changes span across kernel generation, C++ dispatch logic, and Python-level integration. The implementation introduces new utility functions for handling MXFP8-specific weight and scale preparation for Marlin. My review identifies a critical issue in the hardware capability check that could lead to runtime errors on unsupported GPUs.
LGTM

title: [ROCm][perf] Shuffle KV cache to use paged_attention_common
body:
## Purpose For Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507-FP8 model, currently `VLLM_ROCM_SHUFFLE_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT=1` performs worse on small concurrencies, compared to `VLLM_ROCM_SHUFFLE_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT=0`. This PR fixes the issue using `paged_attention_common` from aiter (see https://github.com/ROCm/aiter/pull/1821). ## Test Plan For input and output lengths of 1k and 8k and concurrencies from 8, 18, 32, 64, 128, compare current main branch with and without VLLM_ROCM_SHUFFLE_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT (_vllm_main_shuffle1 and _vllm_main_shuffle0, respectively) to changes of this PR (_pr_shuffle1). Also verified on MI355. Also verified for Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507. ## Test Result For input length 8k and output length 1k (green lines), the changes of this PR (_pr_shuffle1, the solid line) outperform main branch, with or without shuffle kv cache. For input length 1k and output length 8k (orange lines), the changes of this PR (_pr_shuffle1, the solid line) outperform main branch, with or without shuffle kv cache. For input length 1k and output length 1k (blue lines), the changes of this PR (_pr_shuffle1, the solid line) are very close to main branch. This might require further adjustment in aiter `paged_attention_common`. ---
Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist - [x] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)". - [x] The test plan, such as providing test command. - [x] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results - [ ] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model. - [ ] (Optional) Release notes update. If your change is user facing, please update the release notes draft in the [Google Doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YyVqrgX4gHTtrstbq8oWUImOyPCKSGnJ7xtTpmXzlRs/edit?tab=t.0).
author: samutamm

reviews.body:
## Code Review

The pull request updates the AITER branch in the Dockerfile and integrates `aiter.paged_attention_common` for shuffle KV cache handling in `rocm_aiter_fa.py`. This change aims to fix performance issues with small concurrencies for specific Qwen models. The introduction of temporary tensors (`tmp_out`, `exp_sums`, `max_logits`) and new scaling parameters (`K_QScale_hip`, `V_QScale_hip`, `K_QScale_asm`, `V_QScale_asm`) to the `paged_attention_common` function is a significant update to the attention mechanism. I've identified a couple of issues related to variable redefinition and unreachable code that should be addressed.

LGTM. However, the `paged_attention_common` code path is not producing correct output for hybrid models like Qwen3.5.

@tuukkjs can you follow up with a bugfix for this `paged_attention_common`?
When preshuffled is disabled ( the non-shuffled `ROCM_AITER_FA`), `Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-FP8` is generating the correct output with proper lm_eval score.

When we enabled this feature, `Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-FP8` lmeval score is 0.

title: [MoE Refactor] Make SharedExperts class for use with DefaultMoERunner
body:
## Purpose
Move shared experts functionality into `SharedExperts` class.  The `DefaultMoERunner` and modular kernels defer to `SharedExperts` for executing shared experts (making sure that they are executed in the proper spot and only one time).

Built on top of https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/33049

Final PR: https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35949

</details>
author: bnellnm

reviews.body:
## Code Review

The pull request refactors the shared experts functionality into a new `SharedExperts` class, which is then integrated into `DefaultMoERunner` and modular kernels. This change aims to centralize the management and execution of shared experts, ensuring they are handled correctly and only once. The changes involve updating method signatures to reflect the new `SharedExperts` type, removing direct references to `_shared_experts` in `FusedMoE`, and introducing new helper methods in `DefaultMoERunner` for shared expert application and DP chunking. Additionally, a `MemoizingRouter` class is added, and several `TODO` comments are introduced, indicating areas for future improvement or clarification. The overall direction of the refactor is positive, improving modularity and clarity around shared expert handling.

not for this PR, but leaving a thought.

I think one very confusing piece is that we have multiple potential "owners" of the shared expert
- OWNED BY EXTERNAL
- OWNED BY RUNNER
- OWNED BY MK

One way we could deal with this is having the shared expert passed in via `apply()` and then the runner can make the decision dynamically. It might make the code easier to follow.

Not for this PR, but a thought for the future.

title: [Misc] Add 20 regression tests for 11 tool parser bug fixes
body:
## Purpose

Claude Code and I audited recent tool parser bug-fix PRs (Sept 2025 until now) and found that several landed without corresponding test coverage. This is purely additive test coverage to prevent regressions as we refactor, cleanup, and redesign some of these areas.


- Mistral: fast detokenization text detection (PR #37209)
- Qwen3Coder: malformed XML crash, anyOf double-encoding, speculative decode streaming (PRs #36774, #36032, #35615)
- DeepSeekV32: delimiter preservation with fast detokenization, skip_special_tokens adjustment (PR #33964)
- GLM-4 MoE: zero-argument tool calls, transformers 5.x delimiter handling, Unicode character preservation (PRs #32321, #31622, #30920)
- MiniMax M2: anyOf nullable parameter handling for non-null and null values (PR #32342)
- Step3p5: MTP-style variable-chunk and multi-token streaming (PR #33690)
- Kimi K2: native tool call ID extraction and multi-turn ID continuity (PR #32768)

## Test Plan

```
pytest -sv tests/tool_parsers
```

## Test Result

All the new tests passed, and all the old ones continue to pass.

```
510 passed, 1 skipped, 42 xfailed, 2 warnings
```
author: bbrowning

reviews.body:
## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

## Code Review

This pull request introduces a comprehensive suite of regression tests across multiple tool parsers, including DeepSeekV32, GLM4-MoE, KimiK2, MinimaxM2, Mistral, Qwen3, and Step3p5. These tests address various edge cases and potential issues such as delimiter preservation, `skip_special_tokens` logic, handling of zero-argument and malformed tool calls, Unicode character preservation, native tool call ID extraction, `anyOf` nullable parameter parsing, fast detokenization, and streaming behavior with multi-parameter and variable-sized chunks. A review comment highlights a malformed JSON string in a MinimaxM2 test, which needs to be corrected to ensure the test functions as intended.
LGTM, thank you for the thorough work!

title: [KVTransfer] Fix TpKVTopology.is_kv_replicated equality case
body:
## Purpose
This PR fixes the equality case in `TpKVTopology.is_kv_replicated()`.

Previously, the helper also treated `tp_size == total_num_kv_heads` as replicated, while this case should still mean each TP rank owns one distinct KV head rather than a replicated KV-cache layout.

## Changes

- Fix the condition in `TpKVTopology.is_kv_replicated()`
- Add a brief comment.

## Test Result

author: JianDan0212

reviews.body:
## Claude Code Review

This pull request is from a fork — automated review is disabled. A repository maintainer can comment `@claude review` to run a one-time review.

## Code Review

This pull request refines the logic for determining KV cache replication in `vllm/distributed/kv_transfer/kv_connector/utils.py`. The `is_kv_replicated` method now correctly identifies replication only when the number of TP workers (`tp_size`) is strictly greater than the total number of KV heads, clarifying that an equal number of TP workers and KV heads does not constitute replication. No feedback to provide.

## Pull request overview Fixes `TpKVTopology.is_kv_replicated()` to treat the equality case (`tp_size == total_num_kv_heads`) as **not** replicated, aligning the helper with the intended KV head ownership model. **Changes:** - Update replication condition from `>=`-like behavior to strict `>` - Add docstring clarification for the equality case --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
LGTM thanks @JianDan0212

title: (security) Enforce frame limit in VideoMediaIO
body:
- Updated the `load_base64` method in VideoMediaIO to truncate frames exceeding the specified limit, preventing potential memory exhaustion from excessive input.
- Added a new test to verify the enforcement of the frame limit.


## Purpose

The purpose of this MR is to enforce the limit when submitting base64 images in order to avoid DoS vulnerabilities


## Test Plan

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces frame truncation for base64-encoded JPEG videos to prevent potential memory exhaustion, accompanied by a new regression test and test utility refactoring. A review comment points out a logic error in the truncation implementation: using -1 for num_frames incorrectly removes the last frame due to Python's slicing behavior, and a value of 0 leads to a crash in np.stack.

title: [Bugfix][LMCache][KVConnector] fix potential memory leak in LMCache multiprocess mode
body:
## Purpose

  Fix a potential memory leak in LMCache multiprocess (MP) mode caused by lookup locks not being freed for chunks that vLLM computes itself rather than retrieving from LMCache.

  When a prefetch lookup returns hits but vLLM decides to compute some (or all) of those blocks itself, the corresponding LMCache chunk locks were never released. Over time, these leaked locks prevent LMCache from evicting stale entries, leading to unbounded memory growth.

  This fix calls `free_lookup_locks` in `update_state_after_alloc` to release locks on chunks that will never be retrieved by vLLM:
  - If no retrieve is needed (`condition=False`), all locked chunks are freed.
  - If a partial retrieve is needed, only the locks for vLLM-computed blocks are freed; the remaining locks are released later during the actual retrieve. Boundary misalignment between vLLM blocks and LMCache blocks is handled inside `free_lookup_locks` (floor division).

  ## Test Plan

  1. Start LMCache MP server:

      ```bash
      python3 -m lmcache.v1.multiprocess.server --l1-size-gb 100 --eviction-policy LRU
      ```

  2. Start vLLM with LMCache MP connector:

      ```bash
      vllm serve meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct \
          --load-format dummy \
          --max-model-len auto \
          --tensor-parallel-size=1 \
          --gpu_memory_utilization 0.8 \
          --enable-prefix-caching \
          --enable-chunked-prefill \
          --kv-transfer-config '{"kv_connector":"LMCacheMPConnector", "kv_role":"kv_both"}' \
          --port 12345
      ```

  3. Run benchmark:

      ```bash
      vllm bench serve \
          --port 12345 \
          --model meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct \
          --dataset-name prefix_repetition \
          --num-prompts 500 \
          --request-rate 4 \
          --backend openai-chat \
          --endpoint /v1/chat/completions \
          --ignore-eos \
          --prefix-repetition-prefix-len 20000 \
          --prefix-repetition-num-prefixes 200 \
          --prefix-repetition-suffix-len 128
      ```

  ## Test Result

  - **Before**: LMCache memory usage climbs to 1.0 and stays stuck there for long periods, as leaked locks prevent the LRU eviction policy from reclaiming stale entries.
  - **After**: Once usage exceeds the watermark, eviction kicks in immediately and memory usage is relieved. Locks are properly released for vLLM-computed blocks, allowing LRU eviction to function as expected.
author: royyhuang

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request addresses a potential memory leak in LMCache's multiprocess mode by ensuring that lookup locks are properly released. The issue occurred when vLLM computed blocks that were also found in LMCache, but the locks for these blocks were never freed, leading to memory growth. The fix introduces logic in `update_state_after_alloc` to call `free_lookup_locks` for chunks that vLLM computes itself and won't retrieve from LMCache. The implementation correctly distinguishes between cases where a full or partial retrieve is needed and seems to correctly handle the lock releasing logic. The changes look solid and directly address the described problem.
Otherwise LGTM!
LGTM

title: [Perf] triton bilinear_pos_embed kernel for ViT
body:
## Purpose

Add a fused Triton kernel for bilinear position embedding interpolation in ViT (`Qwen3_VisionTransformer`). This replaces ~25 small eager-mode CUDA kernel launches (linspace, meshgrid, indexing, multiply, sum, reshape, permute) with a single fused GPU kernel that performs bilinear interpolation with spatial-merge reordering baked into the index math. This helps to reduce the CPU overhead.

All four Qwen3 VL model variants benefit automatically since they share the same `Qwen3_VisionTransformer` class:
- `qwen3_vl`, `qwen3_5`, `qwen3_vl_moe`, `qwen3_5_moe`

When Triton is unavailable, a pure-PyTorch fallback (`pos_embed_interpolate_native`) is used, preserving the original behavior.

This is part of the efforts of NVIDIA MLPerf Inference Submission V6.0 @wangshangsam .

## Test Plan

```bash
# Accuracy tests (8 grids x 2 dtypes + temporal repeat)
pytest tests/kernels/core/test_vit_bilinear_pos_embed.py -v -s

# Kernel benchmark
python benchmarks/kernels/benchmark_vit_bilinear_pos_embed.py

# End-to-end MM processor benchmark
vllm bench mm-processor \
    --model Qwen/Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct \
    --dataset-name random-mm \
    --num-prompts 20 \
    --num-warmups 3 \
    --random-input-len 512 \
    --random-output-len 128 \
    --random-mm-limit-mm-per-prompt '{"image": 3, "video": 0}' \
    --random-mm-bucket-config '{(256, 256, 1): 1.0}' \
    --metric-percentiles 50,90,99
```

## Test Result

![kernel-comparison-2](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0c50495d-55ea-404d-b5fa-a60cb813de5c)

**Hardware**: NVIDIA GB200, bfloat16

#### Kernel-level benchmark (single grid, `triton.testing.do_bench`)

| Grid | Native (us) | Triton (us) | Speedup |
|------|------------|-------------|---------|
| (1,48,48) | 419 | 8.2 | 51.3x |
| (1,64,64) | 408 | 10.3 | 39.6x |
| (1,128,128) | 427 | 19.2 | 22.3x |

#### End-to-end MM processor benchmark (`vllm bench mm-processor`)

| Stage | Before (ms) | After (ms) | Improvement |
|-------|------------|------------|-------------|
| encoder_forward (mean) | 4.85 | 3.49 | **28% faster** |
| E2E latency (mean) | 763.70 | 728.72 | **4.6% faster** |

#### Accuracy (18/18 tests passed)
- float32: max diff < 5e-5 across all grids
- bfloat16: max diff < 1e-2 across all grids
- Validated against real Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct pos_embed weights
- We didn't see accuracy loss for `nvidia/Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B-Instruct-NVFP4` in the Shopify dataset (during MLPerf Inference VLM submission).

cc: @wangshangsam, @b-mu 

---

- [x] The purpose of the PR
- [x] The test plan
- [x] The test results
author: zhandaz

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request introduces a fused Triton kernel for bilinear position embedding interpolation, which significantly improves performance by replacing numerous small PyTorch operations with a single GPU kernel. The implementation is robust, providing a native PyTorch fallback for environments without Triton. The changes are well-structured, with the core logic cleanly refactored into the new Triton kernel and a corresponding native function. The inclusion of comprehensive accuracy tests and benchmark scripts is excellent, validating both the correctness and the substantial performance benefits of the new kernel. My review of the implementation did not identify any issues of high or critical severity.
Overall LGTM

title: [Core] Simplify multimodal masking
body:
## Purpose

Since PyTorch 2.9.0 (https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/156384) `target[mask] = src` doesn't cause `cudaStreamSynchronize` anymore in cases where `mask` is a CPU tensor.

This PR simplifies `_merge_multimodal_embeddings` by removing the need for `masked_scatter_` without re-introducing a CPU/GPU sync. This also simplifies the model runner since the mask doesn't need to be transferred to the GPU anymore.

## Test Plan

I verified that for Qwen3VL no `cudaStreamSynchronize` ops are visible in the torch profile.

I'm keeping this as a draft for now to see what @DarkLight1337 or @ywang96 think of this approach. I'm happy to then also double check some other multi modal models.

```bash
vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct-FP8 --limit-mm-per-prompt.video 0 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.96

vllm bench serve --backend openai-chat --model Qwen/Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct-FP8 --endpoint /v1/chat/completions --dataset-name hf --dataset-path lmarena-ai/VisionArena-Chat --hf-split train --num-prompts 1000
```
